tasks.title: Replace std::thread::sleep with tokio::time::sleep in concurrent_eval async tests
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-runtime/tests/concurrent_eval.rs`, `test_committed_node_survives_cancellation` uses `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50))` inside an async evaluator body within a `tokio::spawn` task. Under the `#[tokio::test]` current_thread runtime, this blocks the entire executor for 50ms, preventing other tasks from being polled. The test passes by accident due to current_thread serialization, not by design.

**Fix:** Replace `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50))` with `tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await` inside the async `evaluate` fn at line ~2132. This is a one-line change since `AsyncNodeEvaluator::evaluate` is already async.

tasks.title: Reduce mutex acquisitions in ConcurrentScheduler priority sort and cleanup
tasks.description:
Two performance issues in `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`:

1. **Sort acquires mutex O(N log N) times** (lines 285-289): `dirty_nodes.sort_by_key` calls `promoter.effective_priority()` per comparison, each locking the internal mutex. Since nodes are freshly registered and no promotions have occurred, sort directly from `config.node_priorities` — zero mutex acquisitions, same result.

2. **cleanup_level per-node mutex** (lines 349-353): `promoter.remove(node)` acquires and releases the mutex per node. Add a `batch_remove` method to `SharedPriorityPromoter` or lock the inner field once outside the loop.

**Files:** `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`, `crates/reify-runtime/src/priority_promotion.rs`

tasks.title: DRY: compute node_overrides once per node in execute_with_config
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`, `config.node_overrides.get(&node).copied().unwrap_or_default()` is computed twice for each dirty node: once in the dirty/skip pre-computation pass (line ~262) and again in the commitment tracker registration block (line ~298). These will silently diverge if the lookup logic changes.

**Fix:** Compute the override once at the top of the per-node loop and reuse it. Option A: store `(NodeId, NodeCommitmentOverride)` pairs in `dirty_nodes`. Option B: compute and bind `let override_ = ...` once in the is_dirty block and carry it through.

**File:** `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`

A node with this override should always be dropped when cancel fires, regardless of elapsed time. Add `test_always_cancel_when_stale_drops_result`: set a node with AlwaysCancelWhenStale override and CommitmentPolicy with always_commit_after=0ms (would normally commit), fire cancel, assert NOT in changed.

2. **OnlyRunOnFinalInputs with final inputs (false branch)** — `test_only_run_on_final_inputs_skipped` only tests the true case. Add `test_only_run_on_final_inputs_runs_when_final`: give node OnlyRunOnFinalInputs override, set `has_intermediate_inputs` to `|_| false`, assert node IS in changed.

tasks.title: Use JoinSet to abort detached tasks on ConcurrentScheduler error paths
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs` (lines 368-375), when returning Err on panic or cancel paths, remaining JoinHandles in `handles` are dropped. In tokio, dropping a JoinHandle detaches (does not abort) the task — those evaluations continue running in the background holding `Arc<E>` and `Arc<Mutex<CommitmentTracker>>`. The evaluator's side effects continue after the caller believes execution has stopped.

**Fix:** Replace `Vec<JoinHandle>` with `tokio::task::JoinSet`. In error branches, call `join_set.abort_all()` before returning Err. This ensures cooperative cancellation of in-flight tasks and is consistent with the existing cancel semantics.

**File:** `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`

tasks.title: Solver: warm-start convergence robustness improvements
tasks.description:
Address architectural robustness suggestions from task 495 reviews for the warm-start optimization path:

1. **Convergence reporting**: When FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS budget is exhausted without convergence, SolveResult::Solved is returned with no indication of suboptimality. Either add convergence metadata to SolveResult::Solved or check argmin's TerminationReason.
2. **Dimension-scaling FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS**: The flat 500-iteration budget doesn't scale with problem dimensionality. Nelder-Mead needs O(N²) iterations. Scale as `FEASIBLE_OPT_ITERS_BASE * (n_params + 1)` capped at MAX_ITERS. Add a multi-parameter warm-start test (N≥2).
3. **Fallback on drift**: When initially_feasible=true but the optimizer drifts out of the feasible region during the reduced-budget run, fall back to returning the known-feasible initial point instead of Infeasible.

Files: crates/reify-constraints/src/solver.rs, crates/reify-constraints/tests/solver_integration.rs

tasks.title: LSP: Scope member lookups to enclosing declaration
tasks.description:
find_member_decl and compute_goto_definition search ALL declarations for a member name match, returning the first hit regardless of which declaration the cursor is in. In a file with multiple declarations sharing member names (e.g. two occurrences both with `param diameter`), span info can come from one declaration while type info comes from another (find_member_decl), and goto-def always jumps to the first declaration's member (compute_goto_definition).

Fix both:
1. analysis.rs find_member_decl: have find_parsed_member_span_and_doc also return the owning declaration name, then match both template name AND member name in the compiled-module lookup.
2. goto_def.rs compute_goto_definition: check whether the cursor offset falls within the declaration's span and only search members of the enclosing declaration (or fall back to first-found if no enclosing declaration).

Memory hints: search "LSP cross-declaration member lookup scoping" and "find_member_decl ambiguity"

Refactor:
1. Add `pub enum EntityKind { Structure, Occurrence }` in analysis.rs (or a shared types module).
2. Optionally introduce `pub struct EntitySummary<'a> { name, params, lets, constraints, kind: EntityKind }`.
3. Rename `structure_names()` → `entity_names()` or `declaration_summaries()`.
4. Update call sites in hover.rs and completion.rs to match on EntityKind for display strings.

All call sites are within the reify-lsp crate — mechanical rename with no semantic change.

tasks.title: SolveResult API: distinguish fallback-to-initial from genuinely optimized Solved
tasks.description:
SolveResult::Solved now has dual semantics: (1) optimizer converged with genuine objective improvement, or (2) optimizer drifted infeasible and solver fell back to initial feasible point with no objective progress. Callers cannot distinguish these at runtime.

Options:
- (a) Add `SolvedWithWarning { values, reason }` variant that callers treat like Solved but also emit a warning
- (b) Add `objective_optimized: bool` field to Solved
- (c) Extend NoProgress to carry optional fallback values

Option (a) or (b) preferred — keeps the change additive. Update the NOTE comment at solver.rs:639-643 to reflect the actual structural limitation (NoProgress carries no values) rather than claiming it's purely about API stability.

tasks.title: Solver fallback path: defer allocation, extract helper, validate objective
tasks.description:
Three related improvements to the warm-start fallback path in solver.rs:

1. **Defer fallback HashMap** (perf): `initial_fallback_values` HashMap is built eagerly on every warm-start solve but only consumed on the error path. Since `initial: Vec<f64>` stays in scope, defer construction into the `if final_max_residual > FEASIBILITY_THRESHOLD` block. Zero cost on success path.

2. **Extract params_to_value_map helper** (DRY): The fallback block (lines 513-532) duplicates the zip-and-collect from `build_trial_values` (lines 51-67). Extract `fn params_to_value_map(params: &[AutoParam], x: &[f64]) -> HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>` and call from both.

3. **Validate objective on fallback** (correctness): The fallback path (line 602-603) bypasses post-solve objective validation at lines 619-625. If the initial point produces undefined objective (div-by-zero, missing value), fallback silently promotes to Solved. Add the same objective check before returning fallback.

tasks.title: Refactor TauriToolContext constructors to builder pattern + McpConfig struct
tasks.description:
## Problem

TauriToolContext has 4 constructors for 2 optional fields (event_emitter, selection), creating a 2^n combinatorial explosion pattern. The naming is inconsistent (new_with_selection vs with_event_emitter). Additionally, claude_bridge.rs uses a 3-tuple for mcp_config requiring #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)].

The `with_event_emitter()` constructor silently creates a private Arc<RwLock<SelectionInfo>> that is never shared with the frontend — a potential API footgun where selection always returns empty.

## What "done" looks like

1. TauriToolContext uses a builder pattern: `TauriToolContext::builder(engine).with_event_emitter(f).with_selection(arc).build()`
2. A named `McpConfig<F>` struct replaces the 3-tuple in claude_bridge.rs new_inner
3. The type_complexity suppression is removed
4. `with_event_emitter()` is deprecated or removed (callers use builder)
5. All existing tests pass

## Files to modify
- gui/src-tauri/src/mcp_context.rs — replace constructors with builder
- gui/src-tauri/src/claude_bridge.rs — McpConfig struct, remove type_complexity suppression
- gui/src-tauri/src/main.rs — update mcp_tool_call to use builder
- gui/src-tauri/src/tests/mcp_context_tests.rs — update test helpers
- gui/src-tauri/src/tests/mcp_dispatch_tests.rs — update test helpers
- gui/src-tauri/src/tests/claude_bridge_tests.rs — update test helpers

tasks.title: Fix selectionStore: implement immediate selection dispatch or correct misleading comments
tasks.description:
## Problem

The selectionStore.ts createEffect has comments stating "selection (click) updates are sent immediately since they are infrequent" and "send immediately if only selection changed", but the implementation unconditionally routes ALL changes through a 100ms setTimeout — there is no fast path for selection-only changes.

This creates a race window: if an MCP get_selection call arrives within the 100ms debounce after a user click, it reads stale selection state. The documented design intent was never implemented.

## What "done" looks like

Either:
(a) Implement immediate dispatch for selection changes: track previous selectedEntity, call invoke synchronously when only selectedEntity changed, debounce only hover-only changes at 100ms
OR
(b) Accept 100ms is fine for all updates and fix the misleading comments to document actual behavior

Decision: prefer (a) since the original design intent was deliberate — Claude may call get_selection within the same interaction after a user click.

## Files to modify
- gui/src/stores/selectionStore.ts — implement immediate selection dispatch or fix comments
- gui/src/__tests__/selectionStore.test.ts — add test verifying the chosen behavior

### memory_hints

tasks.title: Fix LSP completions and member counting for guarded-group members
tasks.description:
Two related bugs where guarded-group members are excluded from LSP features:
1. member_names() in analysis.rs:138-145 only iterates template.value_cells but the compiler stores guarded members in template.guarded_groups.members/else_members. This means completions (completion.rs:43) miss all params/lets inside where-blocks.
2. structure_names() in analysis.rs:150-177 flat-counts members without recursing into GuardedGroup variants, undercounting params/lets.
Both need to iterate guarded_groups in addition to value_cells/top-level members.

Three related improvements: (1) Replace bare (SourceSpan, Option<&str>) return tuple with a named struct MemberSpanInfo { span, doc } — goto_def.rs already discards the second element. (2) Consider moving find_named_member_span to reify-syntax since it has no LSP dependencies and the compiler's register_guarded_names (lib.rs:4500-4539) duplicates the same recursion pattern. tasks.title: Fix incomplete content_hash in test builder build() methods
tasks.description:
Several test builders in crates/reify-test-support/src/builders/ compute content_hash values that ignore semantically significant fields, which could cause false cache hits in tests exercising the incremental evaluation engine.

Affected builders:
1. **CompiledFieldBuilder::build()** (field.rs:63) — hash uses only `name` + constant `[99]`, ignoring `domain_type`, `codomain_type`, and `source`. Two fields with the same name but different types/sources produce identical hashes.
2. **TraitDefBuilder::build()** (trait_def.rs:68) — requirement hashing uses `std::mem::discriminant(&r.kind)` but ignores the inner type values. Two `Param(Type::Real)` vs `Param(Type::Int)` requirements with the same name produce identical hash contributions.
3. **CompiledTraitBuilder::build()** (trait_def.rs:162-168) — hashes only member names, ignoring requirement kinds entirely.

Fix each build() to incorporate all semantically significant fields into the content_hash, and add tests asserting hash distinctness (e.g., same name + different source → different hash; same requirement variant + different inner type → different hash).

The inline type only includes `selected_entity`, `diagnostics`, `constraints`, missing the `current_file` and `attached_contexts` fields added in task 525. There is also no prompt-building logic for the new fields, so they are silently dropped even though the full stack (bridge.ts → Rust → sidecar types.ts) now transmits them.

Fix:
1. Replace the inline type annotation on handleSendMessage's `context` parameter with `SendMessage['context']` imported from `./types.js`.
2. Add `contextParts.push(...)` branches for `current_file` and `attached_contexts` in the prompt-building section.

1. Add a test: `claudeSendMessage('hello', {})` — verify the current behavior
2. Decide whether `{}` should be treated as no-context (check `Object.values(context).some(v => v !== undefined)`) or left as-is with a documenting test

The Equals<A,B> compile-time assertion in types.typecheck.ts catches structural drift between ClaudeMessageContext and MessageContext, but the runtime field mapping in claudeSendMessage (bridge.ts:130-134) and the mirror mapping in App.tsx (lines 65-68) manually enumerate field names. If a new field is added to both interfaces, the type assertion passes but the runtime mapping silently drops the new field.

Options:
1. Replace explicit field enumeration with a computed mapping (iterate Object.keys + camelToSnake helper)
2. Add an exhaustiveness helper type that asserts every key of ClaudeMessageContext appears in the mapping literal
3. At minimum, add prominent comments in both bridge.ts and App.tsx warning that the mapping must be kept in sync

Also add a comment in App.tsx near the manual mapping (lines 65-68) pointing to types.typecheck.ts so maintainers know to update both places.

tasks.title: Fix compile_geometry_op None placeholder and GeomRef::Step(0) hardcoding bugs
tasks.description:
Two pre-existing bugs in geometry compilation (crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs and crates/reify-eval):

1. **compile_geometry_op None placeholder**: When compile_geometry_op returns None (compilation failure), no placeholder is pushed into step_handles. This causes silent corruption in GeomRef::Step(n) relative indexing — all subsequent step indices are off by one per failed op, leading to wrong geometry references downstream.

2. **compile_geometry_call GeomRef::Step(0) hardcoding**: compile_geometry_call() hardcodes target: GeomRef::Step(0) for all pattern/modify/transform ops. This means all geometry operations target the first step regardless of the intended target geometry.

Fix: (1) Push a sentinel/error placeholder into step_handles when compile_geometry_op returns None. (2) Resolve the correct GeomRef target based on the geometry name→step index map.

tasks.title: Standardize gui/ package manager and commit canonical lockfile
tasks.description:
All JS lockfiles (package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml) are currently gitignored per task 536, meaning no reproducible dependency snapshot exists in version control. Standardize on one package manager and commit its lockfile.

Steps:
1. Decide between npm and pnpm for gui/ and gui/sidecar/ (check which is actually in use, what CI uses)
2. Add `"packageManager": "pnpm@<version>"` (or npm equivalent) to gui/package.json to enforce via Corepack
3. Update gui/package.json scripts: change `npm run` references to match chosen PM (e.g. `pnpm run`)
4. Commit the chosen lockfile (e.g. pnpm-lock.yaml) and remove it from .gitignore
5. Keep the OTHER lockfile format gitignored (e.g. keep ignoring package-lock.json if using pnpm)
6. Handle gui/sidecar/ the same way — commit its lockfile for the chosen PM
7. Update orchestrator.yaml merge commands to use the chosen PM (`pnpm install` vs `npm install`)
8. Simplify .gitignore: use `**/package-lock.json` glob instead of per-directory entries for the ignored format
9. Update tree-sitter-reify/ lockfile handling to match

tasks.title: Harden abort test: enable tokio/time, add startup barrier, cover TaskCancelled branch
tasks.description:
Improve `error_path_aborts_in_flight_tasks` test in `crates/reify-runtime/src/concurrent.rs`:

1. **Enable tokio/time for tests** — Replace `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(3))` with `tokio::time::sleep` to avoid blocking a tokio worker thread. Replace the busy yield loop (`while elapsed < 2s { yield_now().await }`) with `tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2)).await` — each yield is a cancellation point, and sleep is too, but without thousands of unnecessary context switches.

2. **Add startup barrier** — The panic task does a single `yield_now()` assuming slow tasks have started. Add an `Arc<AtomicUsize>` startup counter that slow tasks increment at entry, and have the panic task spin until `startup_counter.load() == 2` before panicking. This removes timing assumptions and prevents vacuous passes.

3. **Add TaskCancelled branch test** — Current test only exercises the `is_panic()` error branch. Add a second test that triggers the `Err(_)` / `SchedulerError::TaskCancelled` branch and verifies sibling tasks are aborted.

tasks.title: orient_quaternion: reject dimensioned Scalar inputs
tasks.description:
orient_quaternion uses as_f64() which silently strips physical dimensions — a LENGTH-dimensioned Scalar (e.g. 1 meter) is accepted as quaternion component w=1.0. This is inconsistent with orient_euler and orient_axis_angle which enforce dimension constraints via trig_input (rejecting non-ANGLE Scalars).

Fix: Add dimension validation before the as_f64() calls in orient_quaternion — reject any argument whose dimension() is not DIMENSIONLESS. Only bare Real, Int, or dimensionless Scalar values should be accepted as quaternion components.

Also update the existing test orient_quaternion_dimensioned_scalar_strips_dimension to assert is_undef() instead of asserting success, since the fix makes dimensioned inputs produce Undef.

Memory hints: search "orient_quaternion as_f64 dimension validation", entities: eval_builtin, orient_quaternion, trig_input

tasks.title: Improve cancelAnimationFrame polyfill fidelity in claudeIntegration tests
tasks.description:
In `gui/src/__tests__/claudeIntegration.test.ts`, `cancelAnimationFrame` is a no-op that doesn't remove callbacks from `rafCallbacks`. This means `cancelAndFlush()` (called by done/error handlers) silently leaves stale callbacks. A regression in cancel logic would go undetected.

**Fix**: Implement `cancelAnimationFrame` to actually remove the callback by id, matching the `id = rafCallbacks.length + 1` allocation scheme. Either use index-based splice or maintain a `Map<number, () => void>` for precise lookup.

tasks.title: PropertyEditor code quality: module-scope QUANTITY_RE + submitValue helper
tasks.description: Move QUANTITY_RE regex to module scope and extract a submitValue helper to fix DRY/double-trim issues in PropertyEditor.tsx.
tasks.details:
## Problems

1. **QUANTITY_RE inside component body** — The regex literal at line 96 is re-allocated on every render. Since PropertyEditor re-renders on every keystroke (via editValue signal), this creates steady GC pressure. Move it to module scope (outside the component function) next to other module-level utilities like `groupByEntity`.

2. **Double-trim / DRY violation** — Both `handleKeyDown` (line 108-114) and `handleBlur` (line 136-144) duplicate the trim-then-submit pattern. Additionally, `isValidValue` already calls `value.trim()` internally (line 99), so every submission path trims twice. Extract a private `submitValue(cellId, rawValue)` helper that trims once, validates the trimmed value, and calls `props.onSetParameter`.

## Files
- `gui/src/panels/PropertyEditor.tsx` — move regex, extract helper
- `gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx` — verify existing tests still pass

## Notes
Tasks 125 (S2) and 126 (commitEdit helper) previously covered the DRY/trim issue but are marked done while the issue persists. This is a follow-up to complete the fix.

- `search(query="PropertyEditor submitValue trim validation pattern", project_id="reify")`
- `search(query="QUANTITY_RE module scope regex", project_id="reify")`

tasks.title: PropertyEditor: reject hex/octal/binary and leading-plus numeric inputs
tasks.description: Number('0x10') → 16 and Number('+5') → 5 both pass Number.isFinite(), so the frontend accepts hex/octal/binary literals and leading-plus forms that the backend parse_value_string will reject or misinterpret.
tasks.details:
## Problem

In `isValidValue` (PropertyEditor.tsx:98-103), the check `Number.isFinite(Number(trimmed))` accepts:
- Hex: `'0x10'` → 16
- Octal: `'0o10'` → 8  
- Binary: `'0b10'` → 2
- Leading plus: `'+5'` → 5

These pass frontend validation and are submitted to the backend, which may reject or silently mishandle them.

## Fix

Add a decimal-only regex guard before or instead of the `Number.isFinite` check:
```typescript
const NUM_RE = /^-?(\d+\.?\d*|\.\d+)([eE][+-]?\d+)?$/;
// ...
if (NUM_RE.test(trimmed) && Number.isFinite(Number(trimmed))) return true;
```

This is the same regex as QUANTITY_RE but without the unit suffix group.

## Files
- `gui/src/panels/PropertyEditor.tsx` — tighten `isValidValue`
- `gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx` — add test cases for '0x10', '0o10', '0b10', '+5'

## Notes
Task 125 (S1) previously covered this exact issue and is marked done, but the fix does not appear in the current code. This is a follow-up to complete that fix.

### memory_hints
- `search(query="PropertyEditor hex octal binary validation Number.isFinite", project_id="reify")`
- `search(query="isValidValue numeric check decimal-only", project_id="reify")`

tasks.title: Add runtime payload validation at Tauri IPC boundary in bridge.ts
tasks.description:
The six `event.payload as Omit<Interface, 'type'>` casts in `subscribeToClaudeEvents` (gui/src/bridge.ts:170-193) are unchecked at runtime. The underlying `event.payload` arrives from the Tauri IPC layer as `Record<string, unknown>`, so if the sidecar sends a malformed payload (null, missing `id`, wrong type for `tool_input`, etc.), the cast silently succeeds and the first property access throws an unhandled runtime TypeError.

**Fix**: Add lightweight runtime type guard predicates for each event payload type (e.g. `isTextDeltaPayload(p: unknown): p is Omit<TextDelta, 'type'>`), or use a thin Zod/valibot schema per event type. Log and drop malformed payloads instead of letting property accesses throw into unhandled territory.

Memory hints:
- Search: "OutboundMessage bridge.ts subscribeToClaudeEvents payload cast"
- Entities: bridge.ts, OutboundMessage, subscribeToClaudeEvents, TextDelta, ToolCall

tasks.title: Improve cli_check.rs test quality: extract helper, strengthen assertions
tasks.description:
Post-merge improvements to crates/reify-cli/tests/cli_check.rs from task 516 review.

Four changes in one pass:

1. **Extract `run_check` helper** — The Command::new + .args + .stdin + .stdout + .stderr + .output + from_utf8_lossy pattern is duplicated verbatim in all 6 tests. Extract:
```rust
fn run_check(fixture: &str) -> (std::process::ExitStatus, String, String) {
    let output = Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_reify"))
        .args(["check", fixture])
        .stdin(std::process::Stdio::null())
        .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
        .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
        .output()
        .expect("failed to execute reify binary");
    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
    (output.status, stdout, stderr)
}
```
Then rewrite each test body to use `let (status, stdout, stderr) = run_check(&fixture_path(...));`.

2. **Add OK assertion to indeterminate test** — `check_indeterminate_constraint_exits_success` should also assert `stdout.contains("OK")` to verify the concrete constraint (thickness > 2mm) is evaluated.

3. **Add explanatory comment** — Before the `stdout.contains("All constraints satisfied")` assertion in the indeterminate test, add: `// INDETERMINATE is non-violating by design (auto params not yet resolved), so the summary still reads "All constraints satisfied".`

4. **Assert stderr is clean** — Add `assert!(!stderr.contains("error:"), ...)` to `check_indeterminate_constraint_exits_success`, matching the pattern used by peer tests.

tasks.title: LSP completion: clean up CursorContext types and redundant logic
tasks.description:
Clean up structural issues in `CursorContext` enum and `determine_context`/`compute_completions` in `crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs`:

1. **Expression::structure_name should be `String`, not `Option<String>`** — `determine_context` returns `TopLevel` when there's no enclosing structure, so `Expression { structure_name: None }` is unreachable. Change to `Expression { structure_name: String }` and remove the dead `push_all_members` fallback branch in `compute_completions`.

2. **Add `object_name: Option<String>` to `DotAccess`** — The variant carries no data, preventing future type-aware member narrowing. Use `find_word_at_offset` in `determine_context` to populate it when scanning backward past the dot.

3. **Simplify redundant Expression detection** — The `contains('=')` + `rfind('=')` + `cursor_in_line > eq_pos` block is triply redundant. `line_prefix` already ends at the cursor, so `contains('=')` alone is sufficient. Simplify to a single check.

4. **DRY: extract shared member-pushing logic** — `push_all_members` and `push_scoped_members` are identical except for the iterator source. Extract a generic `push_members` helper.

5. **DRY: extract shared member-collection logic in analysis.rs** — `member_names` and `member_names_for_structure` duplicate the iteration/collection body. Extract a private `collect_template_members` helper. Also add `break` after the matching template in `member_names_for_structure` since names are unique.

tasks.description:

tasks.title: PropertyEditor: hoist regex constants to module scope and document isFinite guard
tasks.description: Move QUANTITY_RE and NUM_RE from inside the PropertyEditor component body to module-level const declarations. Also add a comment on line 102 explaining why both NUM_RE.test() and Number.isFinite() are needed (the regex passes '1e999' which parses to Infinity).
tasks.details:
## Problem

QUANTITY_RE and NUM_RE are declared inside the component function body (PropertyEditor.tsx:96-97). While SolidJS component functions run once per mount (not per render), the regexes are still scoped locally and recreated for every PropertyEditor instance. They are pure constants with no dependency on props or state.

Additionally, the `NUM_RE.test(trimmed) && Number.isFinite(Number(trimmed))` double-check on line 102 is intentional (catches overflow like `1e999` → Infinity) but lacks documentation, confusing future maintainers.

## Fix

1. Move both regex constants above the component:
```typescript
const QUANTITY_RE = /^-?(\d+\.?\d*|\.\d+)([eE][+-]?\d+)?(mm|cm|deg|rad|m)$/;
const NUM_RE = /^-?(\d+\.?\d*|\.\d+)([eE][+-]?\d+)?$/;

export const PropertyEditor: Component<...> = (props) => { ... };
```

2. Add a comment on the isFinite check:
```typescript
// NUM_RE gates non-decimal literals; isFinite catches overflow (e.g. 1e999 → Infinity)
if (NUM_RE.test(trimmed) && Number.isFinite(Number(trimmed))) return true;
```

## Files
- `gui/src/panels/PropertyEditor.tsx`

## Notes
Source: review suggestions 1/3/6/12 (hoist) and 2/4/7 (document isFinite guard) from task 652 review.

queries: resolution for: PropertyEditor: hoist regex constants to module scope and document isFinite guard

tasks.title: PropertyEditor: expand validation test coverage (blur path, leading-plus variants, acceptance)
tasks.description: Add missing test cases for PropertyEditor numeric validation: blur-path rejection, leading-plus decimal/scientific variants, and negative/leading-dot acceptance tests.
tasks.details:
## Problem

Three gaps in PropertyEditor validation test coverage:

1. **Blur path not tested for rejection cases** — `isValidValue` is called in both `handleKeyDown` (Enter) and `handleBlur`, but the new hex/octal/binary/leading-plus tests only exercise the Enter path. The blur behavior (field reverts silently) is untested for these inputs.

2. **Leading-plus test cases only cover integers** — `+5` and `+0` are tested but `+5.5`, `+.5`, and `+1e3` are not. The decimal and exponent branches of the regex are unexercised for leading-plus rejection.

3. **Acceptance tests missing for negative and leading-dot** — The 'valid number' describe block only tests `'42.5'`. Negative numbers (`'-3'`), leading-dot (`'.5'`), and negative decimals (`'-0.5'`) are never explicitly confirmed as accepted by NUM_RE.

## Fix

In `gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx`:

1. Add a `fireEvent.blur` test case (e.g., for `0x10`) asserting `onSetParam` was not called and input value reverted to `'50'`.

2. Replace `['+0', 'leading plus zero']` with `['+5.5', 'leading plus decimal']` or add `['+1e3', 'leading plus scientific']`.

3. Extend the 'valid number' describe with `it.each` cases for `'-3'`, `'.5'`, `'-0.5'`.

## Files
- `gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx`

## Notes
Source: review suggestions 8/9/10 from task 652 review.

tasks.title: PropertyEditor: add overflow guard on QUANTITY_RE validation path
tasks.description: The NUM_RE path has a Number.isFinite guard catching overflow (e.g. 1e999), but the QUANTITY_RE path does not. An input like '1e999mm' passes QUANTITY_RE and reaches the backend unchecked.
tasks.details:
## Problem

In `isValidValue` (PropertyEditor.tsx:99-104), the numeric path correctly uses `NUM_RE.test(trimmed) && Number.isFinite(Number(trimmed))` to catch overflow. But the quantity path uses only `QUANTITY_RE.test(trimmed)` with no overflow check, so `'1e999mm'` passes validation.

This is pre-existing behavior not introduced by task 652, but the inconsistency was surfaced during review.

## Fix

Add an overflow guard before returning on the QUANTITY_RE path:
```typescript
if (QUANTITY_RE.test(trimmed)) {
  const numPart = trimmed.replace(/(mm|cm|deg|rad|m)$/, '');
  return Number.isFinite(Number(numPart));
}
return false;
```

Or, if backend rejection is the intended design choice, document it with a comment.

## Files
- `gui/src/panels/PropertyEditor.tsx`
- `gui/src/__tests__/PropertyEditor.test.tsx` — add test for '1e999mm'

## Notes
Source: review suggestion 11 from task 652 review.

tasks.title: Fix deterministic recheck test: oneshot pattern, cleanup, yield loop
tasks.description:
The deterministic recheck test `ensure_sidecar_ready_returns_ok_via_recheck_when_ready_during_spawn` (added in task 452 step-1) has several quality issues flagged by 5 reviewers:

1. **Oneshot pattern inconsistency**: Uses `Arc<Mutex<Option<DuplexStream>>>` for `held_writer` (lines 2151-2152) — the exact pattern that task 452 steps 5-6 replaced with `tokio::sync::oneshot::channel` in all other tests. Replace with `let (writer_tx, writer_rx) = oneshot::channel::<DuplexStream>()` and `let _ = writer_tx.send(data_writer)` inside spawn_fn, `let _held = writer_rx.await.ok()` outside.

2. **Missing handle cleanup**: After assertions, add `if let Some(mut h) = sidecar.lock().await.take() { h.kill().await; }` consistent with every other test.

3. **Magic yield ceiling**: The `for _ in 0..100` yield loop (line 2172) has no documentation of why 100 was chosen. The internal chain is exactly 2 tasks deep (reader→state-setter). Either document the ceiling (`// 2-task chain; 10 yields is conservative headroom`) or replace with a short `tokio::time::timeout` spin-yield with explicit deadline.

4. **Task reference in section header**: Remove `(task-452/step-1)` from the section comment — task tracker IDs are transient and meaningless to future readers. Use `// --- deterministic re-check path test ---`.

5. **Silent error discard in make_ready_spawn_fn**: Line 72 uses `let _ = writer_tx.send(data_writer)` — change to `.expect("writer_tx receiver dropped")` since silent discard in test helpers makes failures harder to diagnose.

tasks.title: Fix make_ready_handle latent EOF→Crashed race and doc errors
tasks.description:
The `make_ready_handle()` test helper (claude_bridge_tests.rs lines 31-41) has a latent race condition and misleading documentation:

1. **Latent EOF→Crashed race**: Creates a handle with state=Ready and an empty reader (`b""`). The empty reader immediately hits EOF, triggering the on_exit handler which spawns a task to set state to Crashed (the guard at line 298 skips only for NotStarted, not Ready). Currently safe only on current_thread runtime because uncontested tokio::sync::Mutex::lock() doesn't yield. Any use in a multi_thread test or future code adding a yield before Phase 1's state check would produce intermittent Crashed-instead-of-Ready failures.

   Fix options: (a) Change initial state to `SidecarState::NotStarted` so the on_exit guard prevents the Crashed transition, or (b) Use a live duplex with a ready message like make_starting_handle, or (c) Add a doc warning that this helper is only safe on current_thread runtimes.

2. **Misleading doc comment**: Line 29 says "Suitable for tests that only verify stdin writes or state checks" but `_writer_end` is immediately dropped at line 39, closing the read end — any stdin write will fail with broken pipe. Fix: change doc to "Suitable for tests that verify state without interacting with stdin or the reader task."

tasks.title: Wrap conjugate builtin result in sanitize_value
tasks.description:
The `conjugate` builtin in eval_builtin (lib.rs:489) constructs a new `Value::Complex { re: *re, im: -im, dimension: *dimension }` without wrapping in `sanitize_value`. If the input Complex has NaN or Inf in either field, the poisoned value propagates silently. Every other complex-producing builtin (`phase`, `complex_magnitude`, `complex_add`, `complex_mul`, and now `re`/`im` via task 358) calls `sanitize_value` on output. `conjugate` is the only exception.

Fix: Change the conjugate arm to wrap the result in `sanitize_value(...)`. Add tests for conjugate with NaN/Inf inputs returning Undef.

tasks.title: Extract labels_of_kind test helper in LSP completion tests
tasks.description:
The completion test module in `crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs` repeats the same label-extraction idiom ~33 times:
```rust
items.iter().filter(|i| i.kind == Some(K)).map(|v| v.label.as_str()).collect::<Vec<&str>>()
```

Extract a private helper:
```rust
fn labels_of_kind<'a>(items: &'a [CompletionItem], kind: CompletionItemKind) -> Vec<&'a str> {
    items.iter()
        .filter(|i| i.kind == Some(kind))
        .map(|i| i.label.as_str())
        .collect()
}
```

Then replace all inline instances with `labels_of_kind(&items, CompletionItemKind::VARIABLE)` etc.

tasks.title: Switch complex_abs to use f64::hypot for overflow-resistant magnitude
tasks.description:
Replace `(re * re + im * im).sqrt()` with `re.hypot(im)` in `complex_abs` (crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs). The naive formula suffers premature overflow: any component with absolute value above ~1.34e154 causes Inf even when the true magnitude is representable. For example, `re=1e200, im=0.0` has magnitude 1e200, but the current code returns Undef.

`f64::hypot` internally rescales to avoid intermediate overflow and handles NaN/Inf correctly, so `sanitize_value` still catches genuinely non-finite results.

Also fix the misleading test comment in `complex_magnitude_nan_component_returns_undef` which references "hypot" even though the current implementation doesn't use it. After this change, the comment becomes accurate.

Existing overflow tests (f64::MAX components) will still pass since hypot(f64::MAX, f64::MAX) = f64::MAX * sqrt(2) > f64::MAX = Inf.

Memory hints: search "complex_abs sanitize_value overflow", entities: complex_abs, eval_builtin

Fix: Update make_components_3 to always return Value::Scalar (even for dimensionless), matching eval_div/eval_mul. Apply the same fix to complex magnitude(), .re, .im handlers. Consider a shared helper `fn dimensioned_value(v: f64, dim: DimensionVector) -> Value` that always returns Scalar.

tasks.title: Fix tautological _AssertUnknownPayloadContract type assertion in claudeBridge.test.ts
tasks.description:
The compile-time assertion `_AssertUnknownPayloadContract` at gui/src/__tests__/claudeBridge.test.ts:477-478 is vacuously true. It defines a local `UnknownPayloadEvent = { payload: unknown }` and checks `Equals<UnknownPayloadEvent['payload'], unknown>` — which is `unknown === unknown` by construction. It has no structural link to the actual `EventEntry` type (which is function-scoped inside `subscribeToClaudeEvents` in bridge.ts).

Fix options (pick one):
1. **Remove the assertion** — keep only the comment explaining the design intent. The real regression protection comes from tsc catching invalid `as` casts if the payload type were later narrowed.
2. **Make it meaningful** — extract `EventEntry` to module scope in bridge.ts, export it, then write a real assertion like `AssertTrue<Equals<Parameters<EventEntry[1]>[0]['payload'], unknown>>`.

Option 1 is simpler and sufficient. The comment at line 473-476 already documents the rationale clearly.

tasks.description:
build_solved_values (solver.rs:55) uses Iterator::zip which silently truncates to the shorter slice if params.len() != x.len(). If a solver bug produces fewer solution values than auto-params, entries are silently dropped with no error or warning.

Add `debug_assert_eq!(params.len(), x.len(), "params/x length mismatch: {} vs {}", params.len(), x.len())` before the zip call. Also add a test for the mismatched-length case that documents the contract.

This aligns with the project's "silent defaults" convention — logic errors should be noisy, not silently swallowed.

tasks.title: Validate recovery shape in type_alias error-case tests
tasks.description:
The error-case tests in type_alias_tests.rs (strengthened by task 641) still have assertion gaps around Tree-sitter recovery output:

1. **Dimensional tests** (missing_right_operand, missing_left_operand, missing_both_operands, lines 278-317): Only assert `!errors.is_empty()` but never inspect the recovered TypeAlias when one is produced. A regression that emits a plausible-looking but wrong TypeAlias would pass. Fix: if a TypeAlias is produced, assert `ta.name == "Foo"` and validate that `ta.type_expr` is garbled (e.g., empty name or unexpected structure).

2. **Empty RHS test** (parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic, lines 363-393): The inner assertion `ta.type_expr.name.is_empty() || !errors.is_empty()` is a disjunction that passes whenever errors are present, even if `type_expr.name` is non-empty. The documented invariant ("zero-width recovery node produces empty name") is not actually enforced. Additionally, the branch where `type_expr.name.is_empty() && errors.is_empty()` is permitted — meaning the parser could silently accept `type Foo =` without any error signal. Fix: assert `ta.type_expr.name.is_empty()` unconditionally inside the `if let Some(ta)` branch, and add an unconditional `!errors.is_empty()` assertion at the top level (matching the other error-case tests).

Currently `selectEntity(null)` triggers an immediate dispatch, then `hoverEntity(null)` triggers a debounced dispatch — two backend syncs for one logical operation.

2. **Test**: Add a backend sync test for `clearIfRemoved` inside the existing `describe('backend sync')` block. Set both selectedEntity and hoveredEntity to the same path, call clearIfRemoved, and verify the number and content of invoke calls. This covers the double-dispatch scenario.

tasks.title: Refactor first-pass eval loop: match on cell.kind + merge Param branches
tasks.description:
The first-pass loop over `template.value_cells` in `eval()` (crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs ~lines 970-1088) uses an `if / else if / else if` chain that:
1. Splits the two exhaustive Param sub-cases (with-default and no-default) across separate top-level conditions, hiding that they are sub-cases of one variant.
2. Silently falls through for `ValueCellKind::Let` cells. A future new variant would compile cleanly but be silently skipped.

**Fix:** Restructure to `match cell.kind { ValueCellKind::Auto => { … }, ValueCellKind::Param => { if let Some(ref expr) = cell.default_expr { /* evaluate default, Determined */ } else { /* Undef, Undetermined */ } }, ValueCellKind::Let => { /* deferred to second pass */ } }`. This makes Let-skip explicit, ensures compile-time exhaustiveness for new variants, and makes the two Param sub-cases visually adjacent.

tasks.title: Extract eval journal/cache boilerplate helper on Engine
tasks.description:
The Started→work→Completed journal+cache pattern is repeated 13+ times in crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs with identical boilerplate: `let start = Instant::now()`, two `self.journal.record(EvalEvent{...})` calls, `DependencyTrace::default()`, `self.cache.record_evaluation(...)`. Each call site differs only in the NodeId, the computed value/determinacy, and whether real work runs between the events.

**Fix:** Extract a helper method on `Engine` such as:
```rust
fn record_node_eval<F>(&mut self, node_id: NodeId, version: VersionId, f: F) -> EvalOutcome
where F: FnOnce(&mut Self) -> CachedResult
```
that emits the Started event, runs the closure, calls `cache.record_evaluation`, and emits the Completed event. Migrate existing call sites to use it.

tasks.title: Fix stale cache entry for param nodes after edit_param
tasks.description:
When `edit_param` is called on a param (especially one that was initially Undef/Undetermined), the param's cache entry is never updated — it remains at `CachedResult::Value(Undef, Undetermined)` from the original eval. The param is not in `eval_set` (it's the source of change, not a dependent) and is never marked Pending either. This leaves the cache inconsistent with the snapshot.

**What's wrong:**
1. The param's cache entry retains stale `(Undef, Undetermined)` after edit_param sets it to `(new_value, Determined)`.
2. The param is never transitioned to Pending during edit_param, so its freshness stays Final with old data.
3. Any future code path querying the cache for `NodeId::Value(param_id)` would get stale data.

**Fix:**
1. At the start of `edit_param`, after updating `new_snapshot.values`, also call `self.cache.record_evaluation(NodeId::Value(cell.clone()), CachedResult::Value(new_value.clone(), DeterminacyState::Determined), VersionId(version_id), DependencyTrace::default())`.
2. Optionally call `self.cache.mark_pending(&NodeId::Value(cell.clone()))` before the dependent-marking loop, then update after.

**Test plan:** Add tests for (a) double edit_param on same param, (b) eval-after-edit to verify param reverts or retains correctly.

**Memory hints:** search for "edit_param cache consistency", "cache stale entry", entity names: edit_param, CachedResult, mark_pending

2. Add a new test (or extend existing) that calls `engine.edit_param(n_id, Value::Real(2.0))` after initial eval and asserts `y` becomes `Real(4.0)` with `Determined` determinacy.

tasks.title: Include type_args in TypeParam bound hash and add edge-case tests
tasks.description:
The type_param hash in compute_trait_content_hash (trait_def.rs) only hashes `b.trait_ref.name`, ignoring `type_args`. Two TypeParams with the same bound trait name but different type arguments (e.g., `Rigid<Real>` vs `Rigid<Int>`) produce identical hashes — the exact class of bug task 628 was created to fix.

Fix: Include type_args in the bound string in compute_trait_content_hash, e.g.:
```rust
s.push_str(&format!(":bound:{}:{}", b.trait_ref.name, 
    b.trait_ref.type_args.iter().map(|t| t.to_string()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(",")));
```

Also add edge-case tests:
1. TypeParam `T` with empty bounds vs TypeParam `T` with one bound — assert hashes differ (already covered by existing test, verify)
2. TypeParam `T` with `default: None` vs TypeParam `T` with `default: Some(Type::Real)` — assert hashes differ
3. TypeParam with bound `Rigid<Real>` vs `Rigid<Int>` — assert hashes differ (the main fix)

tasks.title: Wrap clearIfRemoved body in batch() for atomic state updates
tasks.description: In gui/src/stores/selectionStore.ts, clearIfRemoved (lines 92-99) calls selectEntity(null) and hoverEntity(null) sequentially when both match, triggering two separate createEffect runs instead of one. The first run fires an immediate backend dispatch (selectionChanged=true), then the second fires a debounced dispatch (hoverChanged=true). Wrap the body in batch() to collapse into a single effect run with one dispatch, matching the pattern already used in clearHighlights, engineStore, and claudeStore. Flagged by performance reviewer on task 696.

tasks.title: LSP goto-def: configurable stdlib path and DocumentStore integration
tasks.description:
Review follow-ups from task 485 (cross-file goto-definition), grouped by server.rs changes:

**S1: Hardcoded stdlib path** — `server.rs:283` hardcodes `root.join("crates/reify-compiler/stdlib")` as dev-mode only. Accept optional stdlib path via `InitializeParams::initialization_options` (e.g. `{ "stdlibPath": "..." }`), falling back to dev-mode heuristic. ModuleResolver already takes an explicit stdlib_root.

**S3: Unnecessary disk I/O for open documents** — `server.rs:286-290` always calls `std::fs::read_to_string` even for files already in DocumentStore. Snapshot open document contents into a HashMap before entering spawn_blocking, check store first, fall back to disk only for unopened files. Also gives correct results for unsaved editor changes.

tasks.title: Harden stamp-path tests: behavioral assertions, failure-mode coverage, dedup setup
tasks.description: In tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs: (1) Replace source-text assertions in test_stamp_path_is_profile_independent with behavioral tests that construct a stamp path and verify it is src-relative. (2) Fix test_stamp_shared_across_simulated_profiles to actually use separate OUT_DIR-like paths to prove the shared stamp invariant. (3) Add a test for stamp-write failure (read-only stamp path, assert no panic). (4) Extract a shared make_populated_src_dir() helper to eliminate the 5x duplicated setup block.

tasks.title: Stamp write robustness: atomic writes, read-only fallback, cargo:warning
tasks.description: In tree-sitter-reify/build.rs: (1) Replace eprintln with cargo:warning= on stamp write failure so it surfaces in normal cargo output. (2) Use atomic write (write to temp file + rename) to prevent concurrent cargo profiles from reading a partial stamp. (3) Fall back to OUT_DIR stamp if src/ is not writable, preserving correctness in Nix/read-only-CI environments.

tasks.title: LSP goto-def: goto_def.rs code quality improvements
tasks.description:
Review follow-ups from task 485 (cross-file goto-definition), grouped by goto_def.rs changes:

**S2: Redundant iteration** — Phase 0 and Phase 2 iterate declarations separately. Could merge into single pass.

**S4: Substring name search** — `find_name_offset_in_decl` uses `str::find(name)` with no word-boundary check. Could match inside comments or longer identifiers.

**S5: Magic string "_target"** — `find_declaration_in_source` passes literal `"_target"` to `ModulePath::single()`. Should use `module_name_from_uri` helper.

**S6: Fallback offset out of bounds** — `find_name_offset_in_decl` returns unclamped `decl_start` on failure. Should return `decl_start.min(source.len() as u32)`.

**S7: Missing test for ImportKind::Aliased** — `import parts as P` variant has no test. Add test asserting navigation to file start.

**S8: Test boilerplate** — 6+ cross-file tests repeat identical resolver setup. Extract a `parts_resolver(target_source)` helper.

tasks.title: Use stable hash for grammar content hashing
tasks.description: In tree-sitter-reify/build.rs: Replace std::hash::DefaultHasher (output not stable across Rust versions) with a stable hash like FNV-1a or CRC32. This prevents a redundant tree-sitter generate on every toolchain upgrade. Low priority since the consequence is one extra build.

tasks.title: build.rs: replace DefaultHasher with stable hash, fix error handling and ergonomics
tasks.description:
Address 6 suggestion-level review findings from task 564 reviews, all in tree-sitter-reify/build.rs.

1. **DefaultHasher version instability** (architect, robustness, performance reviewers): `content_hash()` uses `std::hash::DefaultHasher` which is not stable across Rust toolchain versions. After a `rustup upgrade`, the stamp hash changes, forcing `tree-sitter generate` on every build until the stamp is rewritten. Replace with a stable FNV-1a hash (manual implementation, no crate needed): `let mut h: u64 = 0xcbf29ce484222325; for b in &bytes { h = h.wrapping_mul(0x100000001b3) ^ (*b as u64); }`.

2. **content_hash panic on missing grammar.js** (architect, robustness): `needs_generate()` calls `content_hash(grammar_path)` which panics if grammar.js is unreadable, but a missing stamp file returns `true` gracefully. Make consistent: return `true` (needs regen) when grammar.js is unreadable, letting `run_tree_sitter_generate()` fail with a clear message.

3. **OUT_DIR fallback to "."** (architect): `std::env::var("OUT_DIR").unwrap_or_else(|_| ".".to_string())` writes the stamp file to the crate root if OUT_DIR is unset. Replace with `.expect("OUT_DIR must be set by Cargo")` since build.rs is only run by Cargo.

4. **needs_generate signature ergonomics** (performance): Takes `&[&std::path::Path]` requiring a double-Vec allocation. Change to `&[std::path::PathBuf]` and call `.as_path()` at point of use, eliminating the intermediate `output_refs` Vec.

5. **Busy poll 100ms granularity** (performance): `run_with_timeout()` polls `try_wait()` every 100ms, adding up to 100ms tail latency after process exit. Reduce to 10ms (two-character change).

6. **Duplicated EXPECTED_OUTPUTS** (reuse): The list `[parser.c, grammar.json, node-types.json]` is hardcoded in both `build.rs` and `scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh`. Consider having build.rs call the script instead of calling tree-sitter directly, or document the sync requirement.

tasks.title: Pipeline test script: fix side-effects, harden assertions, add staleness test
tasks.description:
Address 10 suggestion-level review findings from task 564 reviews, all in tests/infra/test_tree_sitter_pipeline.sh.

**Side-effects and cleanup:**
1. **grammar.js mtime not restored** (architect, robustness, test analyst): `test_auto_generation_rebuilds_parser` does `touch grammar.js` but never reverts the mtime. Save original mtime before touch and restore in cleanup: `orig_mtime=$(stat -c %y "$grammar")` then `touch -d "$orig_mtime" "$grammar"`.

2. **Empty array expansion idiom** (robustness, test analyst): `cleanup()` uses `"${CLEANUP_ACTIONS[@]:-}"` which expands to a single empty string when the array is empty, causing a spurious `eval ""`. Use `"${CLEANUP_ACTIONS[@]+"${CLEANUP_ACTIONS[@]}"}"` (the `+` form) or guard with `[[ ${#CLEANUP_ACTIONS[@]} -eq 0 ]] && return`.

3. **Temp file accumulation** (performance): Assert helpers append cleanup actions for temp files but don't clean up immediately. Add `rm -f "$tmpfile"` at the end of each branch in the helper to scope temp file lifetime to the assertion call.

**Assertion quality:**
4. **Misleading error message** (test analyst): `test_build_rs_no_stdout_inheritance` says "must use .stdout(Stdio::piped()) or .stdout(Stdio::null())" but the sibling deadlock test bans `Stdio::piped()`. Update message to say "must use .stdout(Stdio::null())".

5. **Fragile grep patterns** (test analyst): Both source-grep tests use single-line patterns that break if rustfmt wraps `.stdout(` onto a separate line. Use `grep -Pz` for multiline matching or add a comment in build.rs that the `.stdout(` call must stay on one line.

6. **Incomplete negative test** (test analyst): `test_generate_script_fails_without_grammar` only checks exit code, not error message content. Add `grep -q 'grammar.js' "$tmpfile"` to verify the error mentions grammar.js.

7. **Weak positive test** (test analyst): `test_generate_script_succeeds_normally` uses `assert_file_exists` but not `assert_file_nonempty` for grammar.json and node-types.json. Add `assert_file_nonempty` for all three output files.

8. **Redundant assertion** (reuse): `assert_file_exists` immediately before `assert_file_nonempty` on the same path is redundant (`-s` catches missing files). Remove the redundant `assert_file_exists` in `test_auto_generation_rebuilds_parser`.

**Missing coverage:**
9. **Untested staleness path** (test analyst): The stamp-hash-mismatch branch of `needs_generate()` is never exercised. Add a test: ensure parser.c and stamp exist, corrupt the stamp content, run cargo check, assert parser.c is regenerated and stamp updated.

**DRY:**
10. **Duplicated grep-test boilerplate** (reuse): `test_build_rs_no_piped_stdout_deadlock` and `test_build_rs_no_stdout_inheritance` share ~12 lines of identical structure. Extract helpers like `assert_build_rs_grep_present <pattern> <message>` and `assert_build_rs_grep_absent <pattern> <message>`.

tasks.title: Harden bridge.ts wire mapping type safety: typed return type and literal map values
tasks.description:
Two type-safety improvements to bridge.ts MESSAGE_CONTEXT_FIELD_MAP:

1. **Typed return for mapContextToWire**: Currently returns Record<string, unknown>, erasing all type info about the wire shape. Define a mapped type:
   ```ts
   type WireMessageContext = { [K in keyof Required<MessageContext> as typeof MESSAGE_CONTEXT_FIELD_MAP[K]]: MessageContext[K] }
   ```
   Use it as the return type so callers can statically verify snake_case keys.

2. **Literal union for map values**: MESSAGE_CONTEXT_FIELD_MAP values are plain `string`, so typos in snake_case values (e.g. `selected-entity` instead of `selected_entity`) are not caught by tsc. Narrow the value type to a union of known snake_case literals:
   ```ts
   Record<keyof Required<MessageContext>, "selected_entity" | "diagnostics" | "constraints" | "current_file" | "attached_contexts">
   ```

Both changes make the Rust wire contract explicit in the type system.

tasks.title: Add compile-time exhaustiveness guard for ChatPanel buildMessageContext and fix toStrictEqual
tasks.description:
Two issues from task 611 review:

1. **buildMessageContext() enforcement gap** (ChatPanel.tsx:68): MESSAGE_CONTEXT_FIELD_MAP in bridge.ts is compile-time enforced, but buildMessageContext() in ChatPanel.tsx is only guarded by a comment. Adding a new MessageContext field triggers a tsc error in bridge.ts but gives no signal that buildMessageContext() needs updating. Add a compile-time assertion in types.typecheck.ts that verifies buildMessageContext() return type satisfies Required<MessageContext>, or add a runtime CI test asserting its camelCase keys match Object.keys(MESSAGE_CONTEXT_FIELD_MAP).

2. **toEqual vs toStrictEqual** (claudeBridge.test.ts:183-189): The test "mapContextToWire passes undefined fields through" uses toEqual, but Vitest toEqual ignores undefined-valued properties ({a: undefined} equals {}). Switch to toStrictEqual so the test actually verifies that all snake_case keys are present in the output even when input fields are absent.

tasks.title: Harden tree-sitter-generate.sh: timeout alignment, missing-timeout fallback, shared lib

2. **No timeout fallback (S2)**: When neither `timeout` nor `gtimeout` is found, tree-sitter generate runs without a timeout guard and can hang indefinitely. Add background-process + sleep + kill pattern as portable fallback, or make timeout a hard prerequisite. File: scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh lines 143-148.

3. **compute_hash duplication (S3)**: Identical portable SHA-256 helper exists in both scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh (compute_sha256) and scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh (compute_hash). Extract to shared scripts/lib.sh. Files: scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh lines 31-40, scripts/test_tree_sitter_generate.sh lines 18-27.

tasks.description:

3. Same test: Missing `span.end` upper-bound check. Compute `b_end` and assert `info.span.end <= b_end as u32` to match the thoroughness of `find_member_decl_scoped_to_second_structure`.

4. Missing failure mode test in goto_def.rs: No test for when Phase 2 fallback also fails (identifier exists in no declaration). Add a test where cursor is on an unknown identifier inside a declaration, verify `compute_goto_definition` returns `None`.

tasks.title: Fix usize underflow and escape off-by-one in find_err_arm_braced test helper
tasks.description:
The find_err_arm_braced helper in build_logic_tests.rs has two minor bugs and a test quality gap:

1. **usize underflow (line 259):** `depth -= 1` panics in debug mode if a `}` is encountered at depth 0 (e.g. from an unhandled char literal or comment). Use `depth.checked_sub(1)` and return None on underflow, or switch to isize.

2. **Escape off-by-one (line 248):** Inside the string-scanning loop, `i += 2` for `\\X` escapes. If the backslash is the last byte, `i` overshoots and the outer `i += 1` skips the first byte after the closing quote. Add a bounds check after `i += 2`.

3. **Boundary assertion (line 316-340):** test_try_wait_error_path_kills_child only asserts the extracted arm *contains* child.kill()/child.wait(). Since the Ok(None) arm also has these calls, over-extraction would pass silently. Add `assert!(!err_arm.contains("Ok(None)"))` to verify extraction doesn't bleed into sibling arms.

Memory hint: search for "task 606 brace-depth tracking" for implementation context.

tasks.title: Refactor per-entity solve loop: DRY extraction, determinism, perf, dead code
tasks.description:
The per-entity-group solve loop in resolve_concurrent_edit (lib.rs:700-844) and edit_param (lib.rs:1755-1898) is structurally identical (~130 lines). This task consolidates 12 related review findings from task 491:

**DRY + naming (critical):**
- Extract a private `solve_entity_groups()` helper method that both resolve_concurrent_edit and edit_param call, eliminating the duplicated group-build → filter-constraints → dirty-check → solve → accumulate pattern
- Define a private `struct EntityGroup { params: Vec<AutoParam>, ids: HashSet<ValueCellId> }` to replace the opaque `(Vec<AutoParam>, HashSet<ValueCellId>)` tuple used as HashMap values

**Determinism + correctness (high):**
- Use `BTreeMap<String, EntityGroup>` instead of `HashMap` for `entity_groups` so group solve order is deterministic (affects diagnostic ordering and cross-group value visibility)
- Snapshot `current_values` once before the per-group loop and pass the immutable snapshot to each group's ResolutionProblem, preventing cross-group contamination via iteration-order-dependent resolved values
- Guard `self.eval_state.as_ref().unwrap()` at lib.rs:1864 with proper error handling (early return or propagate error) instead of panicking when eval_state is None

**Performance (medium):**
- Pre-classify constraints by entity in a single O(M × expr_size) pass before the per-group loop, instead of calling extract_dependency_trace per constraint per group (current: O(N × M × expr_size))
- Clone `current_values` and `functions` once before the loop instead of per-group; consider Arc-wrapping functions

**Dead code cleanup (low):**
- Remove `ConcurrentEditSetup.objective` field (line 227) — it is set in prepare_concurrent_edit (line 577) but never read anywhere. With per-entity resolution, a single-entity objective misrepresents the API contract. Remove the field and assignment.

**Memory hints:** search for "solve_entity_groups", "EntityGroup struct", "BTreeMap deterministic"; entity names: resolve_concurrent_edit, edit_param, ResolutionProblem, ConcurrentEditSetup

**Missing output assertions (medium):**

tasks.title: Add solver path diagnostics to verify fallback vs optimization in tests
tasks.description:
Multiple review suggestions identified that warm-start solver integration tests assume the fallback path is triggered without verifying it. If NLopt convergence behavior changes (version update, iteration budget formula change, platform differences), tests could silently switch from testing the fallback path to testing the optimization path and still pass — or vice versa, fail spuriously.

Affected tests:
- `warm_start_falls_back_to_initial_when_optimizer_drifts_infeasible` (exact-value assertion only valid on fallback path)
- `warm_start_fallback_returns_exact_initial_values` (same issue, 3-param variant)

Current state: `SolveResult::Solved` carries no metadata about which code path produced the result. The fallback path logs via `tracing::debug!` but this isn't programmatically accessible.

Proposed fix: Add an optional `diagnostics` or `path` field to `SolveResult::Solved` (or a separate `SolverDiagnostics` struct) that indicates whether the result came from:
1. Early-exit (initial already feasible, no objective)
2. Optimization convergence
3. Fallback to initial (optimizer drifted infeasible)

Then update fallback tests to assert the path taken, not just the values.

Note: `build_solved_values` already has 3 unit tests in solver.rs (lines ~2188-2280) covering the value construction itself. This task is about the integration-level path verification.

Memory hints: search "solver fallback path", search "SolveResult diagnostics", entity DimensionalSolver, entity build_solved_values

This violates the file's established convention of factoring repeated assertion logic into named helpers (assert_malformed_recovers, unwrap_single_type_alias).

tasks.title: Restore error assertion and diagnostics in empty_rhs type alias test
tasks.description:
The parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic test in type_alias_tests.rs has three issues introduced by task 716:

1. **Lost error assertion**: `_errors` is discarded — no assertion that parse errors are produced for malformed input `"type Foo ="`. The task description explicitly asked for an unconditional `!errors.is_empty()` assertion but it was not implemented. Fix: bind as `errors` and add `assert!(!errors.is_empty(), "expected at least one parse error for empty RHS");` before the TypeAlias shape check.

2. **Lost diagnostic info**: The error vector should be included in assertion failure messages for debugging. Thread `errors` into assert format strings.

3. **Fragile .expect()**: The unconditional `.expect()` encodes Tree-sitter recovery behavior as a permanent invariant. Either revert to `if let Some(ta)` with the strengthened assertions inside, or add a comment documenting that the `.expect()` is intentional and tied to the current Tree-sitter grammar/version so future maintainers know the dependency. Also improve the `.expect()` message to include `decls` content via `.unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("expected TypeAlias, got decls={:?}", decls))`.

tasks.title: Replace wildcard arms with explicit leaf variants in LSP member recursion functions
tasks.description:
In `find_named_member_span` and `count_members_recursive` (crates/reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs), the `_ => {}` wildcard arms silently skip any MemberDecl variant that isn't explicitly handled. This is the exact pattern that caused the Port-member bug fixed in task 636 — when a new container variant is added, the wildcard swallows it.

Replace the wildcards with explicit arms for all non-container leaf variants (Sub, Minimize, Maximize, AssociatedType, Connect, Chain, MetaBlock). The compiler's exhaustiveness check will then flag any new container variant at compile time.

Also update the doc comments on both functions to mention Port.members alongside GuardedGroup.members/else_members.

Memory hints: search "MemberDecl variants" in reify-syntax/src/lib.rs, search "find_named_member_span count_members_recursive" in analysis.rs

Task 636 added low-level tests for find_named_member_span with port-internal members, but several coverage gaps remain:

2. No multi-port test: structure with two ports where the target name is only in the second port, verifying iteration past non-matching ports.

3. No shadowing test: same param name at structure top-level AND inside a port body, to document that first-match-wins (top-level) is the intended semantic.

tasks.title: Fix non-deterministic cross-group contamination in resolve_concurrent_edit and edit_param
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs, both `resolve_concurrent_edit` (line ~759) and `edit_param` (line ~1809) have the same bug: per-entity-group solver loops pass `result.values.clone()` / `values.clone()` as `current_values` to each group's ResolutionProblem, but mutate the values map inside the loop when a group solve succeeds. Later-iterated groups see earlier groups' resolved params. Since `entity_groups` is a HashMap, iteration order is non-deterministic, producing different results across runs.

Fix: Snapshot values once before each loop and use `snapshot_values.clone()` in each iteration's ResolutionProblem.

Additionally, replace `.unwrap()` at line ~1864 with `.expect("edit_param requires eval_state")` for consistency with resolve_concurrent_edit's style, even though the early guard at line ~1488 makes it unreachable.

tasks.title: Replace unreachable! with graceful fallback in LSP enclosing-decl handlers
tasks.description:
The `enclosing_decl_name_at` method (analysis.rs:228) and `compute_goto_definition` (goto_def.rs:28) both use `unreachable!("enclosing_decl_at only returns Structure or Occurrence")` in their match arms. If `Declaration` gains new spanned variants (e.g. Function, Constraint), these will panic and crash the LSP process.

Replace with graceful fallback:
- In `enclosing_decl_name_at`: change `_ => unreachable!(...)` to `_ => return None`

tasks.title: Add From<&CompiledUnit> for UnitEntry conversion
tasks.description:
The prelude-unit seeding loop in compile_with_prelude constructs UnitEntry field-by-field from CompiledUnit. This is fragile: if UnitEntry gains a new field, the manual copy will silently compile with a default rather than carrying the field through.

Fix: Implement `From<&CompiledUnit> for UnitEntry` (using `SourceSpan::empty(0)` for span) and use it in the seeding loop. This makes the conversion canonical and ensures new fields are automatically carried through.

Locations: crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:2805-2813 (seeding loop), UnitEntry/CompiledUnit struct definitions.

tasks.title: Prelude unit diagnostic improvements: sentinel span + collision warning
tasks.description:
Two related issues with prelude unit handling:

1. **Misleading error spans**: Prelude units are seeded with `SourceSpan::empty(0)`. When a module-local unit duplicates a prelude name, the "first declared here" label points to byte 0 of the user's file — misleading. Fix: introduce a `SourceSpan::prelude()` sentinel that the diagnostic renderer shows as "defined in stdlib prelude" instead of a raw offset.

2. **Silent cross-module collision**: `seed_prelude_unit` uses last-wins overwrite with no diagnostic when two prelude modules define a unit with the same name. Fix: in compile_with_prelude's seeding loop, detect when `unit_registry.lookup(&cu.name)` already returns Some before calling seed_prelude_unit, and emit a Severity::Warning naming both conflicting prelude modules.

Locations: crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:543-552 (seed_prelude_unit), lib.rs:2800-2816 (seeding loop), SourceSpan definition.

memory_hints: search "SourceSpan definition" and "UnitRegistry seed_prelude_unit".

tasks.title: Deprecate bare compile() or add doc warning about missing stdlib
tasks.description:
The public API now has three entry points: compile() (no prelude), compile_with_prelude() (caller-supplied), and compile_with_stdlib() (full stdlib). The bare compile() silently fails to resolve any standard units — including the nine that were previously hard-coded — making it a trap for callers.

Fix: Either mark compile() with #[deprecated] pointing to compile_with_stdlib, or add a prominent doc warning that compile() is intended only for prelude-module bootstrapping and will not resolve standard units.

Location: crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:2636-2641.

memory_hints: search "compile entry point API" for context on the three-function API.

4. **thou via stdlib**: `thou` is a chain unit (references mm), but no integration test verifies it resolves via stdlib prelude. Add test: `10thou → 0.000254`.

tasks.title: Add BFS termination guard to elaborate_child_lets_only for structural intermediaries
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs` around line 3921, `elaborate_child_lets_only` has a potential infinite loop for structural intermediary templates. The condition `if found_any || entity_template.value_cells.is_empty()` always enqueues children for structural intermediaries (zero value_cells), even when the entity path does not exist in `values`. For any structural template with a guarded sub-component pointing to itself or another structural template, the BFS will enqueue unbounded depth paths without termination.

Fix: Before enqueuing children of a structural intermediary, verify the entity actually exists in the values map (check whether any key with the depth_entity prefix exists in values). Alternatively, add a max_depth parameter (mirroring unfold_recursive_sub's node_budget) or maintain a visited HashSet to detect repeated entity paths.


tasks.title: Fix fallback test to use conditional_expr() builder instead of manual hash construction
tasks.description:
The `undefined_objective_at_fallback_triggers_no_progress` test in solver.rs manually constructs a `CompiledExprKind::Conditional` with `ContentHash::of(&[5])`, but the canonical discriminant for Conditional is `ContentHash::of(&[4])` (as used in `reify-test-support::builders::expr::conditional_expr()`). The wrong discriminant doesn't affect test correctness (solver evaluates `kind`, not `content_hash`) but breaks hash identity convention and could cause issues if hash-keyed lookups are ever involved.

Fix: Replace the 15-line manual `CompiledExpr { kind: CompiledExprKind::Conditional { .. }, .. }` construction (solver.rs ~lines 2422-2434) with a call to the existing `conditional_expr(condition, then_branch, else_branch)` builder from `reify-test-support::builders::expr`. The crate is already a dev-dependency. Also consider adding a `CompiledExpr::conditional()` builder method on the production type alongside the existing `binop`/`literal`/`value_ref` builders.

Memory hints: search "conditional_expr builder", search "ContentHash discriminant Conditional"

tasks.title: Add structured NoProgressKind enum to SolveResult::NoProgress
tasks.description:
SolveResult::NoProgress currently carries only a free-form `reason: String`. Tests that need to distinguish code paths (fallback-point-undefined vs solution-point-undefined vs iteration-limit) must match on substrings like `reason.contains("solution point")`. This couples test correctness to internal prose — any rewording silently breaks path discrimination.

Add a `NoProgressKind` enum (e.g., `FallbackUndef`, `SolutionUndef`, `IterationLimit`, `TooManyUnknowns`, `DidntConverge`) to `SolveResult::NoProgress { kind: NoProgressKind, reason: String }`. Update all construction sites in solver.rs and all test assertions to match on `kind` instead of string fragments. Keep the human-readable `reason` for diagnostics but make `kind` the machine-readable discriminator.

This affects constraint.rs (SolveResult definition) and solver.rs (all NoProgress construction + tests).

Memory hints: search "SolveResult NoProgress", search "solver fallback objective validation"

tasks.title: Add test for successful fallback path (optimizer drifts infeasible, objective defined at initial point)
tasks.description:
Task 625 added tests for both the post-solve and fallback NoProgress paths when the objective is undefined. But the happy path of the fallback mechanism — where the optimizer drifts infeasible, the objective IS defined at the initial feasible point, and the solver returns `SolveResult::Solved { values: fallback }` (solver.rs ~lines 651-658) — has no test coverage. This is the primary success path of the fallback mechanism.

Add a test that constructs a problem where: (1) the feasible region has a defined objective, (2) the infeasible region has a better objective value, (3) the optimizer drifts infeasible chasing the better value, and (4) the solver correctly falls back to `SolveResult::Solved` with the initial feasible values. Assert that returned values match the initial point, not the infeasible optimizer result.

Memory hints: search "fallback objective validation", search "optimizer drifts infeasible"

tasks.title: Fix remaining silent-default eval_arg in Primitive/Modify arms of compile_geometry_op

Add test coverage for the present-but-Undef edge case.

No unit test covers the three-way combination of Satisfied + Violated + Indeterminate entries in report_constraint_results (crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs ~line 397+). The existing tests cover: all-satisfied, satisfied+violated, indeterminate-only, and violated+indeterminate, but not the most likely real-world scenario where all three states coexist.

Add a unit test with three entries covering all three Satisfaction states:
- Assert result == ConstraintOutcome::SomeViolated
- Assert output contains "OK <label>" for the satisfied entry
- Assert output contains "VIOLATED <label>" for the violated entry
- Assert output contains "INDETERMINATE <label>" for the indeterminate entry

tasks.title: Fix test temp dir naming and RAII cleanup in LSP integration tests
tasks.description:
In crates/reify-lsp/src/server.rs, replace all std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("reify-lsp-*-{}", std::process::id())) patterns with either the tempfile crate's TempDir (preferred — cleans up on drop even on panic) or a random suffix (e.g. subsec_nanos()). This fixes: (a) intra-process thread-level collision when two tests share the same name prefix, (b) leaked directories when assertions panic before remove_dir_all, and (c) cross-process CI matrix collision when two binary invocations share the same PID. Affects at least four tests: goto_definition_uses_custom_stdlib_path, goto_definition_prefers_document_store_over_disk, goto_definition_resolves_imported_symbol_across_files, concurrent_goto_definition_completes_without_stalling.

tasks.title: Strengthen test-utils.test.ts: microtask positive case, multi-macrotask drain, try/finally cleanup
Tighten VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE assertions and extract CONSTRAINT_INDENT constant
tasks.description:
Task 803 tightened OK assertions to include the two-space indent prefix (`"  OK "`) matching the production format string `writeln!(out, "  {} {}", status, label)` at main.rs:314, but left VIOLATED and INDETERMINATE assertions without the prefix. This creates an asymmetry: OK assertions verify indentation while VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE assertions don't.

## Changes needed

### 1. Add two-space prefix to all VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE assertions

**Unit tests in `crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs`** (~8 locations):
- Line 465: `"VIOLATED clearance"` → `"  VIOLATED clearance"`
- Line 476: `"INDETERMINATE load"` → `"  INDETERMINATE load"`
- Line 493: `"VIOLATED thickness"` → `"  VIOLATED thickness"`
- Line 498: `"INDETERMINATE tolerance"` → `"  INDETERMINATE tolerance"`
- Line 529: `"VIOLATED clearance"` → `"  VIOLATED clearance"`
- Line 534: `"INDETERMINATE thermal"` → `"  INDETERMINATE thermal"`
- Line 563: `"VIOLATED torque_limit"` → `"  VIOLATED torque_limit"`
- Line 597: `"VIOLATED size_bound"` → `"  VIOLATED size_bound"`

**Integration tests in `crates/reify-cli/tests/cli_check.rs`** (~6 positive assertions):
- Lines 46, 96, 132, 136, 168: add `"  "` prefix to VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE positive assertions

**Integration tests in `crates/reify-cli/tests/cli_build.rs`** (~6 positive assertions):
- Lines 34, 112, 158, 163, 190: add `"  "` prefix to VIOLATED/INDETERMINATE positive assertions

Note: negative assertions like `!stdout.contains("VIOLATED")` should remain without the prefix (they check for _absence_ of any VIOLATED line).

### 2. Extract CONSTRAINT_INDENT constant

Near `report_constraint_results` in `crates/reify-cli/src/main.rs`:
```rust
const CONSTRAINT_INDENT: &str = "  ";
```
Use it in the `writeln!` format string and reference it in test assertions via `format!("{}OK stress_limit", CONSTRAINT_INDENT)` or similar.

memory_hints: search "report_constraint_results format" for production format context

tasks.title: Compiler: emit diagnostic for unresolved port member types
tasks.description:
In reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:4542, `resolve_type_name(&type_expr.name).unwrap_or(Type::Real)` silently defaults unresolvable type names to Real without emitting a diagnostic. This is inconsistent with the equivalent code at lib.rs:6185 which properly emits `Diagnostic::error("unresolved type: ...")` before defaulting to Real.

The fix should follow the pattern at line 6185: use `unwrap_or_else` to push a diagnostic error with the source span before falling back to Type::Real. The span is available from `type_expr.span`. This ensures users get an error when writing `param x: UnknownType` in port declarations.

tasks.description:

1. **Deduplicate `build_nested_guarded_members`** — identical helper exists in both `crates/reify-syntax/tests/member_span_tests.rs:176` and `crates/reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs:1398`. Extract to a shared location (e.g. `reify-test-support` or a `#[cfg(test)]` module in reify-syntax).

The boundary test in the same file correctly uses the constant. If `MAX_MEMBER_NESTING_DEPTH` is ever increased above 33, this test silently breaks.

3. **Add Port recursion path test** — The Port arm in `count_members_recursive_depth` (analysis.rs:335-338) recurses with depth+1 but no test exercises this path. Add a test using `MemberDecl::Port` wrapping nested members.

4. **Add partial-counting test** — No test verifies that shallow members are still counted when deep siblings are truncated. Build a flat member list with a Param at depth 2 alongside a GuardedGroup nested beyond MAX_MEMBER_NESTING_DEPTH, assert the shallow param is counted `(1, 0, 0)` while the deep one is not.
