tasks.title: Add tracing::warn when MemberDecl depth limit truncates traversal
tasks.description:
When `count_members_recursive_depth` and `find_named_member_span_depth` hit the depth limit, they silently return `(0,0,0)` / `None`. For `count_members_recursive`, this means incorrect counts propagate to LSP hover output (`EntitySummary.params/lets/constraints`) with no indication of truncation.

Add `tracing::warn!(depth, "count_members_recursive: depth limit exceeded, subtree skipped")` (and analogous for `find_named_member_span_depth`) inside the depth-guard branch before returning. This preserves stack-overflow protection while making truncation observable in LSP server logs.

This aligns with the project convention against silent defaults (feedback_silent_defaults_pattern).

Files: `crates/reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs:302-304` and `crates/reify-syntax/src/lib.rs` (find_named_member_span_depth)

Memory hints: search "silent defaults" for project convention

tasks.title: Replace compiler unwrap_or(Type::Real) with diagnostics (13 locations)
tasks.description:
Convention violation: reify-compiler/src/lib.rs has 13 instances of .unwrap_or(Type::Real) or .unwrap_or(Type::String) when scope.resolve() or resolve_type_name() fails during the second compilation pass. The two-pass compilation guarantees names are registered in pass 1 — a miss in pass 2 is a compiler logic bug that should surface as a diagnostic, not silently assign Type::Real.

Each site should either emit a Diagnostic::error and use a sentinel type, or panic with an ICE message (internal compiler error) since the condition should be unreachable.

Locations: lines 2337, 2504, 2705, 2727, 2759, 2763, 2856, 4559, 4666, 4959, 6352, 6581, 6589

tasks.title: Reconcile infer_type() empty-collection defaults with compiler
tasks.description:
Convention violation + cross-module inconsistency. Value::infer_type() in reify-types/src/value.rs defaults empty List/Set to Type::Int (lines 504, 512) and empty Map keys to Type::String (line 520). The compiler in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs defaults empty List/Set to Type::Real (lines 2705, 2727) and empty Map values to Type::Real (line 2763). This means compile-time and runtime type inference disagree for empty collections.

Fix: either make both consistent (both Type::Real or both Type::Int), or — better — emit a diagnostic / return Option<Type> for the ambiguous case rather than silently picking a default. Task #368 (done) fixed the builders.rs path but missed these value.rs sites.

Locations:
- crates/reify-types/src/value.rs:504, 512, 520
- Also line 524: Value::Option(None) defaults to Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Bool))

memory_hints: search("infer_type empty collection type default convention", project_id="reify")

tasks.title: Guard collection count re-elaboration against Undef count cells
tasks.description:
In reify-eval/src/lib.rs:2059-2090, the collection count re-elaboration phase defaults missing count cells to Value::Undef. When new_count_val != old_count_val, the match arms at lines 2074 and 2090 treat non-Int values (including Undef) as count=0 via `_ => 0`. If the count cell simply hasn't been evaluated yet during incremental re-eval, this destructively removes all collection sub-instances then recreates them from scratch.

Fix: skip the re-elaboration if either count is Undef (cell not yet evaluated), or at minimum log a warning. The `_ => 0` arms should not silently treat Undef as zero count.

Locations:
- crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs:2059, 2065, 2074, 2090

tasks.title: Fix unsanitized NaN/Inf propagation in reify-expr method paths
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs`, the method-call branches for `magnitude` (line 1159), `re`, and `im` (line 1203) call `Value::from_component` without wrapping in `sanitize_value`. The stdlib equivalents (`re(z)`, `im(z)`, `magnitude(z)`) do use `sanitize_value`. This means `z.re` on a Complex carrying NaN returns `Real(NaN)` while `re(z)` returns `Undef` — violating the project convention that logic errors should be noisy.

Fix: add `sanitize_value(...)` wrappers around the `Value::from_component` calls in all three method-call branches (magnitude, re, im) in eval_method_call.

tasks.title: Investigate to_bits().cmp() ordering correctness for negative floats in Value Ord
tasks.description:
The Value Ord implementation uses f64::to_bits().cmp() for comparing float components (Orientation at value.rs:1278-1283, and likely other float-bearing variants). IEEE 754 to_bits() does NOT preserve ordering for negative values — the sign bit inverts the magnitude comparison. For example, -0.5.to_bits() > 0.5.to_bits() even though -0.5 < 0.5, and -1.0.to_bits() > -0.5.to_bits() even though -1.0 < -0.5.

Investigate whether mathematical ordering is required (vs just any consistent total order for BTreeMap keys), and if so, replace to_bits().cmp() with a sign-aware total-order comparison (e.g. the float_ord crate pattern or manual sign-bit fixup). Add tests with negative float components to validate.

Memory hints: search "Value Ord implementation to_bits" and "float comparison convention".

queries: resolution for: Investigate to_bits().cmp() ordering correctness for negative floats in Value Ord

tasks.description:

Any log aggregation or filtering keyed on a `cell_id` field silently stops working.

Fix: Change add_pattern_to_builder (and add_auto_coord) to return a richer error type that carries cell_id separately, so solve() can log it as a structured field:

Alternatively, a simpler approach: define a small `BuilderError { cell_id: ValueCellId, message: String }` struct used as the error type for add_auto_coord → add_point → add_pattern_to_builder, then destructure at the solve() call site.

tasks.title: Add body-parity diff check to sync_comments_test.sh
tasks.description:
The sync_comments_test.sh only verifies that `// SYNC:` comment markers exist — it does not verify that the two sanitize_value function bodies are actually identical. A developer could modify one implementation (e.g., add a Value::Integer arm to one crate but not the other), leave the SYNC comment in place, and the test would still pass.

Add a second check that extracts both function bodies and diffs them. Example approach using awk:
```bash
extract_fn() { awk '/^fn sanitize_value/,/^}/' "$1"; }
diff <(extract_fn "$EXPR_FILE") <(extract_fn "$STDLIB_FILE") \
  || { echo 'FAIL: sanitize_value bodies differ'; FAIL=$((FAIL+1)); }
```

This turns the test into an actual parity guard, not just a comment-presence check.

After task 835, `engine.rs` directly imports `reify_mcp::DiagnosticInfo`. This couples the engine's domain layer to MCP wire/protocol types. Previously, the architecture maintained a seam: engine.rs produced GUI-local types, mcp_context.rs mapped them to MCP types.

**Evaluate whether** DiagnosticInfo, SourceLocationInfo, and similar types should be moved to the existing `reify-types` crate (or a new `reify-domain` crate) so both `reify-mcp` and `gui/src-tauri/engine.rs` depend on a shared lower-level crate rather than engine depending on MCP.

**Considerations:**
- reify-types already exists and is used by engine.rs

tasks.title: Add error guards to sync_comments_test.sh for missing files and source failures
tasks.description:
Add two robustness improvements to tests/sync_comments_test.sh:

1. **Source error handling (line 15):** Change `source "$REPO_ROOT/tests/infra/test_helpers.sh"` to include an error handler: `source "$REPO_ROOT/tests/infra/test_helpers.sh" || { echo "ERROR: failed to source test_helpers.sh"; exit 1; }`. Currently if test_helpers.sh exists but fails to parse/execute (syntax error, permission denied), `source` silently returns non-zero and the script continues with `assert` and `test_summary` undefined, producing unhelpful "command not found" errors.

2. **File-existence guards (before line 18):** Add guards for the target files before the assert calls:
   ```bash
   [ -f "$EXPR_FILE" ] || { echo "ERROR: $EXPR_FILE not found"; exit 1; }
   [ -f "$STDLIB_FILE" ] || { echo "ERROR: $STDLIB_FILE not found"; exit 1; }
   ```
   The shared `assert` helper redirects stderr to /dev/null (`"$@" >/dev/null 2>&1`), so if either file is missing, grep's "No such file or directory" is swallowed and the test reports a generic FAIL with no indication the file itself is missing.

This follows the existing guard pattern already used on line 14 for test_helpers.sh itself.

**Scope:**

tasks.title: Add path normalization to scrollToLocation file guard
tasks.description:
The `scrollToLocation` effect in Editor.tsx:251 uses strict `!==` string equality to compare `location.file` against `props.store.state.activeFile`. This can silently swallow scroll requests if the backend emits paths in a different format (e.g. `file://`-prefixed URIs vs bare paths).

The codebase already has a precedent: `gotoDefinition.ts:106-108` uses an `isSameFile` check with `.endsWith()` fallback for URI/path format mismatches. The scrollToLocation guard should use consistent path comparison.

**Changes needed:**
1. In `gui/src/editor/Editor.tsx:251`, replace strict `!==` with a normalized path comparison (either reuse `uriToPath` from task 127, or extract a shared `isSameFile` utility)
2. Add JSDoc to the `scrollToLocation` prop (Editor.tsx:23) documenting the file-match behavior: "Scroll the editor to the given location. No-op if location.file does not match the currently active file."
3. Add test in `gui/src/__tests__/Editor.test.tsx` with a `file://`-prefixed path that matches the active file, asserting scroll succeeds

tasks.title: Refine resolve_source contract, allocations, and key derivation in engine.rs
tasks.description:
Consolidate several review findings from task 836 into a single improvement pass on `gui/src-tauri/src/engine.rs`:

1. **Unify resolve_source failure semantics** — The method returns `Option<(String, &str)>` but the `None` arm of `module_name` panics via `unreachable!()`. This hybrid contract (graceful via `?` for source_map miss, panic for module_name miss) is incoherent. Preferred fix: change signature to return `(&str, &str)` with `debug_assert!(self.compiled.is_some())` at entry, making the precondition explicit in the type. Alternative: convert `unreachable!` to `return None` for fully graceful semantics.

2. **Optimize allocations** — `resolve_source` allocates a new `String` via `format!("{}.ri", name)` on every call. Use `HashMap::get_key_value` to return `(&str, &str)` instead of `(String, &str)`, eliminating per-call allocation. Also eliminate the per-diagnostic `file_path.clone()` in `get_diagnostics` (line 315).

3. **Extract module_key helper** — `format!("{}.ri", module_name)` appears in 3 places (load_from_source:81, update_source:180, resolve_source:236). Extract a private `fn module_key(name: &str) -> String` to DRY this up.

tasks.title: Add NaN/Inf guard to conjugate method in reify-expr
tasks.description:
The `conjugate` method in reify-expr (line ~1217) constructs `Value::Complex { re: *re, im: -im, dimension }` without any NaN/Inf guard. If the input has NaN/Inf components, the output is a poisoned Complex. The reify-stdlib counterpart (task 667) was already fixed to wrap conjugate output in sanitize_value, but reify-expr's copy was missed.

With the Complex arm now removed from reify-expr's sanitize_value (task 860), wrapping in sanitize_value won't help. Instead, add a direct non-finite guard before the Complex construction — similar to the phase method's pattern at line ~1190: check for NaN/Inf in re/im and return Undef early.

This is a pre-existing gap widened by the Complex arm removal.

tasks.title: Add Orientation arm to reify-stdlib sanitize_value and add tests
tasks.description:
The SYNC comment at reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs line 1442 says both copies of sanitize_value should stay in sync. Task 866 added a Value::Orientation{w,x,y,z} arm to reify-expr's sanitize_value to catch NaN/Inf quaternion components, but reify-stdlib's copy (lines 1443-1456) still lacks this arm — the wildcard `_ => v` silently passes through pathological Orientation values.

tasks.title: Harden WarnCountingSubscriber: debug_assert, factory usage, comment precision
tasks.description:
Three related improvements in `crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs`, all from post-merge review of task 924:

1. **Add debug_assert in `event()`** (line 67): The defensive level guard was removed in task 924. Restore it as `debug_assert_eq!(event.metadata().level(), &tracing::Level::WARN, "event() reached with non-WARN — enabled() contract violated")` to catch misuse when `WarnCountingSubscriber` is called directly (not through the dispatcher). Aligns with project "silent defaults" convention.

2. **Use `warn_counting_subscriber()` factory in `error_events_rejected_by_enabled_filter` test** (lines 147-154): Replace direct `super::WarnCountingSubscriber::new(Arc::clone(&warn_count))` with `warn_counting_subscriber()` to match the pattern in `non_warn_events_are_not_counted`. The factory returns the shared Arc, making the direct construction unnecessary and removing the only remaining call-site that reaches into the private constructor.

3. **Clarify dispatcher-contract comments** (lines 67, 85-90): Attribute the `event()`-only-called-when-`enabled()`-returns-true guarantee to the tracing dispatcher contract, not to `enabled()` itself. E.g.: "The tracing dispatcher only calls `event()` when `enabled()` returned `true`; our `enabled()` accepts only WARN, so only WARN events reach here."

tasks.title: Harden orientation assertion macros: tolerance API, test coverage, macro hygiene
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 923 review. Seven suggestions across 5 reviewers, all in crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs:

2. **Degenerate negative-sign test** (test_analyst): `sign_insensitive_macro_negative` uses (-1,0,0,0) where x/y/z are trivially zero. Add a test with a fully non-trivial quaternion (e.g. negated 45-degree rotation).

3. **Per-component diagnostic only validates w:** (test_analyst): Add catch_unwind tests for x, y, z component labels too.

4. **Rejection test ignores message** (test_analyst): `sign_insensitive_macro_rejects_wrong_value` only checks `is_err()`. Inspect the panic message content.

5. **Non-Orientation arm untested** (test_analyst): Add a catch_unwind test passing a non-Orientation value to the sign-insensitive macro.

6. **$tol multiple evaluation** (robustness): Macro evaluates `$tol` 9 times. Capture into a local binding for hygiene.

7. **Shared inner helper macro** (reuse_auditor): Both macros repeat per-component abs-diff logic. Extract a private helper macro.

tasks.title: Extract parse_params<T> helper to DRY bridge.rs deserialization
tasks.description:
The two-line pattern `let p: T = serde_json::from_value(params).map_err(|e| format!("<name> params error: {e}"))?;` appears 7 times across all match arms in `InProcessLsp::handle_request` (initialize, initialized, didOpen, didChange, didClose, completion, hover, definition).

Extract a module-private helper:
```rust
fn parse_params<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(params: serde_json::Value, label: &str) -> Result<T, String> {
    serde_json::from_value(params).map_err(|e| format!("{label} params error: {e}"))
}
```

Then each arm becomes `let p = parse_params::<InitializedParams>(params, "initialized")?;` — one line, no format strings scattered across 7 sites. Adding future arms becomes trivially DRY.

Memory hints: search "bridge.rs handle_request deserialization", search "parse_params helper DRY"

tasks.title: Improve bridge.rs breaking-change docs and initialized test consistency
tasks.description:
Three small documentation and test consistency improvements for the `initialized` notification contract in bridge.rs:

1. **Extend breaking-change doc section** (bridge.rs:97-103): The `# Breaking change` doc section only mentions `initialize` but `initialized` also changed behavior (from silently constructing `InitializedParams {}` and discarding caller params to strict deserialization). Add: "The `initialized` notification previously ignored its `params` argument entirely, constructing `InitializedParams {}` unconditionally. It now performs strict deserialization and returns `Err` if `params` cannot be parsed into `InitializedParams`."

2. **Add initialize call in test** (in_process_bridge.rs:404-420): The `initialized_with_malformed_params_returns_error` test doesn't call `initialize` first, unlike every other test that exercises `initialized`. Add `lsp.handle_request("initialize", json!({"capabilities": {}})).await.expect("initialize should succeed");` before the malformed params call for consistency.

3. **Document stable error prefix contract** (in_process_bridge.rs:397-402): The test doc comment should explicitly state that the prefix `"initialized params error"` is part of the public error contract. Callers may match on this prefix to identify deserialization failures.

Memory hints: search "bridge.rs breaking change doc", search "initialized test contract"

tasks.title: SCC design coherence: extract validation, early-return on dup, label dedup, naming
tasks.description:
Review suggestions S1/S2/S3/S4/S5 from task 362 review.

S1 (scc.rs:21-28): Duplicate-template diagnostic uses internal indices instead of span labels. Add TopologyTemplate.span field so diagnostic can use "first defined here" / "duplicate defined here" labels.
S2 (scc.rs:15-34): detect_recursive_structures conflates validation + cycle detection. Extract validate_template_names() or rename function.
S3 (scc.rs:81-107): Rename scc_set to scc_member_set or cycle_member_indices.
S4 (scc.rs:19-29): Early-return vec![] after duplicate-name errors instead of continuing with corrupted graph.
S5 (scc.rs:91-102): Label generation iterates raw sub_components (not deduped). Either document the asymmetry or dedup labels.

tasks.title: SCC minor perf: share HashSet, pre-allocate labels Vec
tasks.description:
Review suggestions S6/S7/S8 from task 362 review.

tasks.title: Clean up solvespace.rs test helper suggestions (S2-S6 from task 855 review)

tasks.title: Fix Value::Real time-bombs in #[ignore]'d self_keyword_eval tests
tasks.description:
Three #[ignore = "requires task 153"] tests in crates/reify-eval/tests/self_keyword_eval.rs match dimensioned scalars against Value::Real(f64), but the codebase stores 5mm/3mm/7mm as Value::Scalar { si_value, .. }. When these tests are un-ignored they will panic with cryptic mismatches. Task 153 is marked done but the tests remain ignored — fix the patterns first so they can be enabled cleanly.

Affected tests at lines 80, 126, 173:
- self_param_eval_produces_correct_value
- self_in_let_arithmetic_eval
- self_in_constraint_eval_satisfied (x-value assertion)

Replace `Value::Real(t)` / `Value::Real(v)` match arms with `Value::Scalar { si_value: t, .. }` / `Value::Scalar { si_value: v, .. }`. Reference patterns: incremental.rs:1097 and occurrence_eval.rs:56 use `Value::Scalar { si_value, .. } if (*si_value - 0.005).abs() < 1e-10`.

Also: change the `1e-15` tolerance check at line 92-95 to `1e-9` to match the surrounding assertions and prevent fragility from float operations in the eval path.

Once the patterns are correct, also remove the #[ignore] guards if task 153's compiler support is genuinely complete.

Source: review escalations esc-154-29, esc-154-30 (auto-dismissed apr 1, never triaged).

queries: resolution for: Fix Value::Real time-bombs in #[ignore]'d self_keyword_eval tests

tasks.title: Fix QualifiedAccess substitute_expr correctness + add coverage
tasks.description:
substitute_expr for QualifiedAccess in crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:4423 currently recurses into the qualifier. EnumAccess (the analogous static-path operation) intentionally clones strings without recursing. With the current behaviour, a value binding named `Foo` in scope will silently rewrite the qualifier in `Foo::bar`, almost certainly producing wrong ASTs in parameterised structure instantiation. This bug has zero test coverage.

## Correctness fix
In substitute_expr, the QualifiedAccess arm should clone qualifier/member opaquely without recursing, matching the EnumAccess pattern. Apply the equivalent fix to InstanceQualifiedAccess (which currently has no substitution coverage either).

If dynamic-qualifier dispatch is intentional, add a doc comment + a covering test; otherwise the recursion is a real bug.

## Type::Error fallback (crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs:3099)
The compile-time fallback for an unsupported QualifiedAccess form emits a "not yet supported" diagnostic and returns CompiledExpr::literal(Value::Undef, Type::Real). Replace `Type::Real` with `Type::Error` (or whichever sentinel exists) to suppress cascading type-mismatch diagnostics. Honours the silent-defaults convention.

## Tests
1. Add a substitute_expr test that puts an `Ident` into bindings and verifies QualifiedAccess.qualifier is NOT replaced (regression for the fix).
2. Add a compiler diagnostic test asserting `Foo::bar` produces exactly one error diagnostic.
3. Add an instance_qualified_access test for chained inner paths: `obj.(A::B::c)`, asserting the nested QualifiedAccess shape is preserved.
4. Add member-span tests for QualifiedAccess and InstanceQualifiedAccess (parallel to member_span_tests.rs).
5. Tighten the existing tautological assertion in parse_invalid_instance_qualified_numeric (line ~148) — currently asserts a 3-way disjunction that almost any outcome satisfies. Pin one expected outcome.

## Lowering cleanup (crates/reify-syntax/src/ts_parser.rs:2147)
The second `matches!` validation block in lower_instance_qualified_access is unreachable dead code (the prior CST kind guard already ensures the result). Replace with a debug_assert! (or remove) and add a ParseError push before returning None for the silent-failure case at lines 2148-2150.

Source: review escalations esc-365-18, esc-365-22 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

tasks.title: Numerical gradient: hot-loop allocs, dim recompute, and edge-case tests
tasks.description:
compute_numerical_gradient_at_point in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs is called per-sample in a hot eval path. Per-axis it currently allocates 4 fresh Vecs (cloned coord vecs + arg vecs), recomputes f_plus.dimension(), and has an unwrap() that can panic. It also has zero coverage for the single-Point calling convention, Composed/Imported source variants, NaN/Inf points, and mishandles the Tensor coord-extraction arm.

## Performance fixes (around lib.rs:684, 737, 753)
1. Allocate `work_coords: Vec<f64>` and `work_args: Vec<Value>` ONCE before the loop. In each axis iteration: perturb in-place, build args via clear() + extend(), restore. Reduces from O(n) per axis to O(1) per axis.
2. Hoist `make_arg` closure outside the for-i loop (it only captures domain_dim: Copy).
3. Pass `grad_dim: DimensionVector` (or codomain_type) as a parameter so f_plus.dimension() is not recomputed per-iteration.
4. Replace `gradient_components.into_iter().next().unwrap()` (line ~753) with .unwrap_or(Value::Undef) to remove the latent panic site.

## Robustness
5. Remove `Value::Tensor` from the coord-extraction match arm at line ~637 (or replace with explicit Value::Undef). A Tensor is logically multi-dimensional; treating it as a flat coord vector is mathematically wrong (would compute Jacobian-of-vectorised-elements, not gradient).
6. Add #[cfg(debug_assertions)] warning before the loop when !single_point_param && params.len() != n, so arity mismatches stop being silent Undef.

## Missing tests (crates/reify-expr/tests/gradient_tests.rs)
7. gradient_3d_field_single_point_param: lambda with 1 Point param and n=3 (currently 0 coverage of single_point_param=true).
8. gradient_imported_field_returns_undef (mirror of gradient_sampled_field_returns_undef).
9. gradient_composed_field_returns_field (Composed source whitelisted but untested — silent regression risk).
10. gradient_sample_with_nan_point_returns_undef and _with_inf_point_returns_undef — exercises the is_finite() guards.

Convention 'silent defaults forbidden' applies — changes (5) and (6) honour it.

Source: review escalation esc-281-66 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

tasks.title: point/vector binop: missing length-mismatch, Undef, zero-divisor tests
tasks.description:
componentwise_binop in crates/reify-expr has guarded paths for empty vectors, length mismatch, Sub right-side Undef, Scalar-zero divisors, Point dispatch length mismatch, and -0.0. None are exercised. The Int(0) division tests pass but related guarded code paths are silently uncovered.

Add targeted tests in crates/reify-expr/tests/point_vector_eval_tests.rs:

1. value_empty_vector_add_returns_undef — exercises the `if a.is_empty() { return Value::Undef }` guard in componentwise_binop.
2. value_vector_with_undef_component_sub_right_propagates — Undef on the RIGHT operand of Sub (existing test only covers LEFT).
3. value_vector_div_scalar_zero_returns_undef — uses `Value::Scalar { si_value: 0.0, .. }` as divisor to verify the as_f64() == 0.0 guard catches Scalar zero (currently only Int(0) and Real(0.0) tested).
4. value_point3_add_point2_mismatched_length_returns_undef and/or value_point3_sub_vector2_mismatched_length_returns_undef — Point dispatch arm length mismatch (existing test only covers Vector).
5. value_vector_div_negative_zero_returns_undef — pin -0.0 divisor behaviour.

## Cleanup
Deduplicate the existing near-duplicate test pairs (value_vector_div_int_zero_returns_undef vs value_vector_div_zero_returns_undef, and the Point variant) by extracting `fn assert_vector_div_zero_returns_undef(zero: Value, zero_ty: Type)` and driving Real(0.0) and Int(0) variants through it.

Source: review escalations esc-458-33, esc-458-35.

queries: resolution for: point/vector binop: missing length-mismatch, Undef, zero-divisor tests

tasks.title: tree-sitter-generate.sh: stale-lock window regression + kill-fallback cleanup
tasks.description:
Unifying MAX_WAIT_SECS=120 in scripts/tree-sitter-generate.sh for both the mkdir-lock attempt count and the stale-age threshold collapsed the original 75s/120s safety buffer to zero — stale recovery now never fires for locks that became stale during our wait. Separately, the kill-based timeout fallback orphans `sleep 60` subprocesses (reparented to PID 1) and lacks SIGKILL escalation if tree-sitter generate ignores SIGTERM.

## Stale-lock regression (the central issue, lines 84, 93)
Restore independent thresholds: keep MAX_WAIT_SECS=120 for flock timeout AND for the stale age comparison, but use a separate MAX_LOCK_ATTEMPTS=75 for the mkdir poll loop count. The current code makes both equal to 120, eliminating the original ~45-second safety buffer where a process started near the same time as our wait could legitimately hold the lock for ~120s without being recovered as stale. Also: change `if [ "$_lock_age" -gt $MAX_WAIT_SECS ]` to `-ge` so a lock exactly at the threshold is recoverable.

## Kill-fallback orphans + signal escalation (line 148)
In the kill-based timeout subshell (`( sleep 60 && kill $_gen_pid ) &`), capture the sleep PID inside the subshell so cancellation can target both the subshell and its sleep child. Or use process-group termination (`set -m; ...; kill -- -$_timer_pid`).

After SIGTERM, sleep briefly and SIGKILL if tree-sitter generate is still alive (`kill $_gen_pid; sleep 2; kill -9 $_gen_pid 2>/dev/null || true`). Also remove partial output files (parser.c, grammar.json, node-types.json) on the timeout error path to prevent leaving an unfinished parser.c on disk.

## .gitignore
Add `tree-sitter-reify/src/.generate.lock.d` (or the glob `tree-sitter-reify/src/.generate.lock*`) to .gitignore so the mkdir lock directory cannot leak as untracked state on POSIX systems where the script crashes mid-lock.

Source: review escalation esc-751-42 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

queries: resolution for: tree-sitter-generate.sh: stale-lock window regression + kill-fallback cleanup

tasks.title: portable_timeout: false-positive race + flag-file POSIX-fallback test gap
tasks.description:
Race in scripts/lib_portable.sh:66 POSIX-fallback timer subshell: `touch flag; kill cmd_pid` runs as separate non-atomic operations, so a command that exits naturally just before kill can be misreported as a timeout. Separately, the new flag-file mechanism's happy path is entirely untested — the existing test suite only covers GNU timeout/gtimeout (which never reach the POSIX flag-file branch on Linux CI) and the mktemp-fail degraded path. False-positive _PORTABLE_TIMEOUT_TIMED_OUT under GNU timeout is also undocumented.

## Race fix (lib_portable.sh:66-82)
Reorder the POSIX-fallback timer subshell so kill runs first and touch only fires if the kill succeeded against a still-live process:
```
( sleep "$seconds" && kill "$cmd_pid" 2>/dev/null && touch "$timeout_flag" 2>/dev/null )
```
This closes the false-positive window where a successful command exits just after touch but just before kill.

## Cleanup trap
Register a local cleanup trap for $timeout_flag covering EXIT/INT/TERM so signals during the multi-second sleep window don't leak temp files in $TMPDIR.

## Documentation
Add an inline comment in the GNU timeout/gtimeout branches (lines 45-46 and 50-52) noting that _PORTABLE_TIMEOUT_TIMED_OUT may be a false positive when a wrapped command naturally exits 124 — the flag is only fully reliable on the POSIX flag-file path. Reflect this in the function-level doc comment so callers know.

## Tests (tests/infra/test_portable_timeout.sh)
1. POSIX-flag-file happy-path test: strip timeout/gtimeout from PATH (mirror Test 8) AND run a slow command so the timer genuinely fires; assert _PORTABLE_TIMEOUT_TIMED_OUT=true and exit code 124. This is the only missing branch for the new mechanism.
2. Reset-semantics test: call portable_timeout with a slow command (timeout → true), then immediately with `true` (success), and assert _PORTABLE_TIMEOUT_TIMED_OUT=false afterward.
3. Stub-tree-sitter test for tree-sitter-generate.sh: stub it to exit 124 with _PORTABLE_TIMEOUT_TIMED_OUT=false and verify the script does NOT emit the 'timed out' error message — exercises the guarded condition added by the fix.

Source: review escalation esc-794-83 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

queries: resolution for: portable_timeout: false-positive race + flag-file POSIX-fallback test gap

tasks.title: bridge.rs initialized arm violates documented param-validation contract
tasks.description:
The bridge.rs doc comment promises 'Notifications do not silently succeed on bad params; the error is propagated to the caller', but the `initialized` arm in crates/reify-lsp/src/bridge.rs:117-119 hardcodes `InitializedParams {}` and discards `params`. `handle_request("initialized", json!(42))` returns Ok(Value::Null) instead of Err(...) — directly contradicting the contract. The malformed-params notification test only covers didOpen, so this divergence is invisible.

## Fix the contract divergence
In crates/reify-lsp/src/bridge.rs:117-119, deserialize params for `initialized` to match all other notification arms:
```rust
let _: InitializedParams = serde_json::from_value(params)
    .map_err(|e| format!("initialized params error: {e}"))?;
server.initialized(InitializedParams {}).await
```
Alternatively, if treating `initialized` as exempt is intentional, explicitly carve it out in the doc comment.

## Tests (crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs)
1. Add `notification_initialized_with_malformed_params_returns_error` mirroring the existing didOpen test (or, if exempt path is chosen, document and assert the actual Ok(Value::Null) behaviour).
2. Add malformed-params tests for `textDocument/didChange` and `textDocument/didClose` (asymmetric coverage today — only didOpen is exercised).
3. Add a `shutdown_returns_ok_null` test (currently shutdown arm has zero coverage).
4. Tighten `notification_with_malformed_params_returns_error` to assert the error string contains "didOpen params error".
5. Tighten `unsupported_method_returns_error` to assert the error message contains "unsupported LSP method:".

Source: review escalation esc-868-50 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

queries: resolution for: bridge.rs initialized arm violates documented param-validation contract

tasks.title: find_named_member_span: depth-limit observability + cleanup duplicated tests
tasks.description:
Depth-limit returns silent None — LSP go-to-def fails with no log signal. analysis.rs still has duplicated depth-limit tests after the function moved to reify-syntax, and the duplicated tests hardcode `33` instead of MAX_MEMBER_NESTING_DEPTH+1. Test coverage gaps for combined Port+GuardedGroup recursion and first-match-wins ordering.

## Observability (crates/reify-syntax/src/lib.rs:210)
Add `tracing::warn!(name, depth, "find_named_member_span: depth limit exceeded")` at the depth-limit return site so silent LSP go-to-def failures are diagnosable in server logs.

## Cleanup duplication (crates/reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs:1333-1393)
1. Delete the depth-limit tests (find_named_member_span_succeeds_within_depth_limit, find_named_member_span_returns_none_beyond_depth_limit, helper build_nested_guarded_members) from analysis.rs. The authoritative copy now lives in crates/reify-syntax/tests/member_span_tests.rs and the analysis.rs copy hardcodes 33 instead of MAX_MEMBER_NESTING_DEPTH + 1.
2. Move the `pub use reify_syntax::{find_named_member_span, MemberSpanInfo}` re-export from analysis.rs:5 to crates/reify-lsp/src/lib.rs (the layering owner), or import directly from reify_syntax in goto_def.rs.

## Tests (crates/reify-syntax/tests/member_span_tests.rs)
3. Add let_without_doc_returns_none_doc (mirror of the existing param_without_doc_returns_none_doc).
4. Add a combined Port-inside-GuardedGroup test exercising shared-depth-counter behaviour across both recursion arms.
5. Add a first-match test where the same name appears at top level and inside a nested GuardedGroup; assert the top-level (earlier byte offset) is returned.

Source: review escalation esc-601-98 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

tasks.title: in_process_bridge: warn-counter helper extraction + assertion robustness
tasks.description:
warn-count delta pattern (`load(Relaxed) - before`) is duplicated 3+ times in crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs with bare usize subtraction (panics in debug if a future refactor decrements the counter). The capabilities test now mixes warn assertion with capability shape (two concerns). Memory-ordering uses Relaxed which is unsafe if the test is ever switched to multi_thread tokio executor.

## Helpers
1. Extract `fn assert_warn_delta(counter: &Arc<AtomicUsize>, before: usize, expected: usize, msg: &str)` — replaces 3 inline copies. Use saturating_sub(before) so a counter regression yields a clean assertion failure rather than a debug panic.
2. Extract `fn install_warn_counter() -> (Arc<AtomicUsize>, impl Drop)` combining the subscriber + drop guard setup repeated in 3+ tests.
3. Move warn_counting_subscriber (and the new helpers) to reify-test-support so other LSP test files (lifecycle.rs, interactive_edit_loop.rs) can share them.

## Test concerns
4. Split initialize_with_malformed_params_still_returns_capabilities into two: one focused purely on capabilities, one on warn count. The current test fails both ways if a future change emits an additional WARN for a legitimate reason.
5. In malformed_initialize_supports_downstream_operations, change `assert_eq!(delta, 1)` to `assert!(delta >= 1)` for resilience to additional warn events.

## Memory ordering
6. Use Ordering::Release for WarnCounter::event's fetch_add and Ordering::Acquire for the assertion loads, so the pattern is robust to a future `flavor = "multi_thread"` switch.

Source: review escalation esc-852-77 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

tasks.title: build_logic_tests.rs meta-test cleanup: off-by-one + duplication + path
tasks.description:
tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs has an off-by-one in fn-body extraction that silently truncates by 1 byte (latent trap if guard pattern moves to function end). Two source-inspection tests redundantly read the same 800-line file from disk in parallel. Both use a hardcoded relative path `tests/build_logic_tests.rs` which breaks if cargo test is invoked from a non-default directory.

## Off-by-one fix (lines ~721-724)
The `fn_section[1..].find(...)` returns an offset relative to the sub-slice but is then used as an index into fn_section, making fn_body 1 byte short. Change to:
```rust
let fn_end = fn_section[1..].find("\n#[test]").map(|i| i + 1).unwrap_or(fn_section.len());
```

## Path robustness
Replace the hardcoded "tests/build_logic_tests.rs" in both test_unix_permission_tests_have_root_guard and test_readonly_guard_drop_logs_error with `concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/tests/build_logic_tests.rs")` to make the tests directory-independent.

## Merge duplicate file reads
Merge both source-inspection assertions into a single test function that reads the file once. They run concurrently today and read the growing 800+ line file twice.

## Strengthen drop test
In test_readonly_guard_drop_logs_error, tighten the assertion from a bare contains("eprintln!") to also require contains("if let Err") (or the specific 'failed to restore permissions' message), so an unrelated debug eprintln does not satisfy the check.

Source: review escalation esc-783-53 (auto-dismissed, never triaged).

queries: resolution for: build_logic_tests.rs meta-test cleanup: off-by-one + duplication + path

tasks.title: Sidecar errorMessage divergence + Editor.test scrollToLocation hardening
tasks.description:
Two GUI/sidecar follow-ups from review backlog:

## (1) Sidecar errorMessage missing plain-object branch
gui/sidecar/src/utils.ts:errorMessage uses the legacy single-line implementation with no plain-object .message handling. gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts has the richer 3-branch implementation. Structured error objects (e.g. from IPC) reaching the sidecar will become '[object Object]'.

Apply the same plain-object handling to gui/sidecar/src/utils.ts:errorMessage that exists in gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts. Either copy the 3-branch implementation, or extract a shared helper into a common module both packages import (preferred).

Add vitest cases for: `{ message: null }` falls through to String() → '[object Object]', `{ message: '   ' }` returns '   ' (whitespace passthrough), and the bare object case.

Source: esc-671-34 / esc-671-36.

## (2) Editor.test scrollToLocation: weak assertions + misleading comments
In gui/src/__tests__/Editor.test.tsx, the mismatch test asserts `head > 0` instead of the deterministic `head == 9`, so a regression that jumps to the wrong position would still pass. Comment misattributes head to column.

- Replace `expect(view.state.selection.main.head).toBeGreaterThan(0)` with `expect(view.state.selection.main.head).toBe(9)` (deterministic from end_column=10, 0-indexed). Also assert anchor === 4.
- Fix the misleading comment: head is computed from end_column, not column.
- Split the dual-purpose 'mismatch is a no-op' test into two named tests: one positive same-file case, one mismatch.

Source: esc-898-51.

queries: resolution for: Sidecar errorMessage divergence + Editor.test scrollToLocation hardening

tasks.title: Small test hardening: type_alias empty-RHS + deferred() reject + null-params
tasks.description:
Three small test-strengthening tasks bundled (low individual cost):

## (1) type_alias_tests: assert documented invariant for empty-RHS recovery
crates/reify-syntax/tests/type_alias_tests.rs:parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic comment explicitly states `errors` is expected to be empty (Tree-sitter silent recovery), but never asserts it. A future grammar change emitting parse errors for `type Foo =` would go undetected.

Immediately after capturing errors, add:
```rust
assert!(errors.is_empty(), "expected no parse errors for empty-RHS recovery, got: {errors:?}");
```

Source: esc-758-40.

## (2) deferred() helper: missing reject() argument coverage
gui/src/__tests__/test-utils.test.ts: deferred<T>().reject takes `reason?: unknown`. Current coverage only tests reject(new Error(...)). Edge cases like reject() with no argument and reject('string') are unverified.

Add tests for:
- d.reject() with no argument → `await expect(d.promise).rejects.toBeUndefined()`
- d.reject('string reason') → rejects with the string primitive

Source: esc-813-19 / esc-813-23.

## (3) lsp_request_impl null-params test: avoid vacuously-passing if-let
gui/src-tauri/src/tests/lsp_bridge_tests.rs:lsp_request_impl_accepts_valid_json_null_literal wraps its body in `if let Err(ref e) = result { ... }`. If result is Ok(_), zero assertions execute and the test passes silently.

Make the assertion unconditional:
```rust
let err = result.unwrap_err();
assert!(!err.contains("invalid JSON params"), "null literal should not trigger a JSON parse error, got: {err}");
```

Add a test case for `""` (empty string params, a realistic accidental input).

Source: esc-869-84.

tasks.title: Gradient: typed-doc + Arc lambda for hot-path field clones
tasks.description:
compute_gradient stores the original field via Box::new(field_val.clone()), deep-cloning the entire captured environment per gradient(). With repeated gradient() calls on the same field this is O(env_size). The lambda field's contract is also documented as 'lambda or Undef' but FieldSourceKind::Gradient now stores a Field there — making the variant a hidden encoding protocol.

## What
1. Update the doc comment on Value::Field.lambda in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs:51 to describe all three valid contents: Value::Lambda (Analytical/Composed), Value::Undef (Sampled/Imported), or Value::Field (Gradient — the original field).
2. Update the FieldSourceKind::Gradient doc comment to describe provenance only — drop references to 'lambda slot' and 'sample handler' that leak reify-expr internals into reify-types.
3. Consider switching Value::Field's lambda from Box<Value> to Arc<Value> so gradient wrapping becomes a refcount bump rather than a deep clone of captured environment. This is cross-crate and may need its own subtask.
4. Document the single_point_param calling-convention contract on Value::Field so it isn't an undocumented heuristic.

Source: review escalation esc-281-66, suggestions about Field.lambda.

tasks.title: Guard cell unwrap_or(Value::Undef) in reify-eval: decide and document
tasks.description:
crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs has 2 instances of `values.get(&g.guard_cell).cloned().unwrap_or(Value::Undef)` for guard cells:
- Line 1767 (initial guard_state_hash computation)
- Line 2042 (edit_param guard fingerprint recomputation in topology_fingerprint update)

These use the silent default pattern that violates feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md. The original integration review (esc-review-20260401T113255-25) flagged a third site that has since been removed/refactored.

## Design question
During the FIRST evaluation pass, guard cells may not have been evaluated yet, so Undef is the correct 'undetermined' state. But during re-evaluation (line 2042 — edit_param path), all guard cells SHOULD have values from the prior snapshot.

Decide one of:
- (a) Replace `.unwrap_or(Value::Undef)` on line 2042 with `.expect("guard cell must be present after initial evaluation")` — and keep line 1767 as-is with an explicit comment exempting it.
- (b) Both sites are correct as-is, but each needs an explicit comment explaining why Undef is the correct fallback (when can guard_cell legitimately be missing here?).

Investigate what happens when a guard cell is missing during re-evaluation in practice — does it currently cause silent wrong topology fingerprints? Add a regression test for whichever direction is chosen.

## Related convention
feedback_silent_defaults_pattern.md: Errors must be noisy — never silently swallow logic errors with unwrap_or(Value::Undef). Recent fix in commit 3c99ec707 removed a similar silent default from Pattern eval_arg.

Source: integration-review escalation esc-review-20260401T113255-25 (auto-dismissed when orchestrator restarted).

tasks.title: Add NaN/Inf pre-guard to conjugate Complex method
tasks.description: In crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs conjugate method (~line 1235-1247), add `if !re.is_finite() || !im.is_finite() { return Value::Undef; }` to the Complex arm before constructing the result. Currently `Complex{re: NaN, im: 1.0}.conjugate()` returns `Complex{re: NaN, im: -1.0}` instead of `Value::Undef`, propagating poison values. This is the same class of bug fixed in task 951 for neg_scalar — follow the same pattern as the phase method guard at lib.rs:1214. Add a unit test for `Complex{NaN, finite}.conjugate() == Undef` and `Complex{Inf, finite}.conjugate() == Undef`.

tasks.title: Hot-path allocation & FP-restore fixes in compute_numerical_gradient_at_point
tasks.description:
Three related implementation fixes in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs around the gradient hot loop:

1. Eliminate the redundant `coords` Vec (line ~778): read `coord_i = work_coords[i]` before perturbation instead of maintaining a separate clone. Saves one O(n) allocation.
2. Add `point_scratch: Vec<Value>` buffer (lines ~793, 803): reuse it across axis iterations instead of allocating a fresh inner Vec inside each `Value::Point(...)` call, reducing the single_point_param path from O(n²) to O(1) inner-Vec allocations.
3. Replace arithmetic restore `work_coords[i] += h` (line ~810) with `work_coords[i] = coord_i` — eliminates ~4 ULP round-trip accumulation that contaminates subsequent axis iterations in the single_point_param path.

Memory hints: search for "compute_numerical_gradient_at_point" and "gradient hot loop allocation" for prior context. Source: task 992 review suggestions [3], [2], [11].

tasks.title: Resolve test-merge structural inconsistency in build_logic_tests.rs
tasks.description:
The diff for task 999 merged test_unix_permission_tests_have_root_guard and test_readonly_guard_drop_logs_error into test_source_self_inspection but left test_is_root_uses_libc_not_raw_ffi as a standalone — a half-applied merge with no principled justification.

Choose ONE of two directions and apply consistently in tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs:

**Option A (split — preferred if CI failure attribution and test isolation are priorities):**
- Restore the original two separate tests with their original names (test_unix_permission_tests_have_root_guard, test_readonly_guard_drop_logs_error).
- Apply the env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR") path fix to both.
- Leave test_is_root_uses_libc_not_raw_ffi unchanged.

**Option B (complete merge):**
- Absorb test_is_root_uses_libc_not_raw_ffi into test_source_self_inspection as a third guarded section (with a `// --- libc invariant ---` comment), reusing the source binding already in scope.
- Remove the standalone test.
- Update the comment to accurately reflect that all three invariant checks share one file read.
- Rename the merged function to enumerate invariants, e.g. test_source_invariants_root_guard_drop_logging_libc_ffi.

After choosing, also update the function name (if option B) to enumerate the invariants it covers.

tasks.title: Add negative-line and character-offset guards to gotoDefinition.ts
tasks.description: In `gui/src/editor/gotoDefinition.ts` same-file branch (around line 115): (1) Before the existing line-bounds check, add `if (location.range.start.line < 0) return;` to explicitly reject malformed LSP responses with negative line numbers (currently they pass the guard and fall through to `doc.line(0)` throwing, producing a confusing warning). The two guards can be combined: `if (location.range.start.line < 0 || location.range.start.line + 1 > view.state.doc.lines) return;`. (2) After `doc.line()` succeeds (line 116), add a character bounds check before computing `targetPos`: `if (location.range.start.character > targetLine.length) return;` (where `targetLine.length = targetLine.to - targetLine.from`) to prevent a stale character offset from silently placing the cursor in the wrong position.

tasks.description:
Two precision improvements to tests/sync_comments_test.sh extraction pipelines:

1. **SYNC: colon delimiter** (lines 29, 41): Change `grep 'SYNC'` to `grep 'SYNC:'` in both extraction pipelines. The file header (line 5) documents the convention as `// SYNC:`, and the assertion greps on lines 18-25 already use `SYNC:.*reify-stdlib::`. The bare `SYNC` pattern could theoretically match "ASYNC" or other words containing the substring.

2. **Function definition boundary** (lines 36, 48): Change `grep -q "fn ${_expr_ref_fn}"` to `grep -qE '^(pub )?(fn|async fn) '${_expr_ref_fn}'\\b'` (or similar definition-anchored pattern). tasks.title: gotoDefinition test hygiene: drop dead mock, tighten anchor assertion, clarify wrapper
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1033 review triage (esc-1033-1). Three small test-hygiene cleanups in `gui/src/__tests__/gotoDefinition.test.ts`:

(S1) In the 'line-bounds guard' > stale-response test (~lines 263-301), the `onNavigate = vi.fn()` mock is declared and passed to `reifyGotoDefinition` but never asserted on (the vacuous assertion was removed in Task 1033). Drop the unused `onNavigate` mock entirely — call `reifyGotoDefinition(currentUri)` instead. This removes dead code and matches the documented test intent.

(S5) In the 'line-bounds guard' > exact-boundary test (~lines 303-344), the assertion uses `selection: { anchor: expect.any(Number) }`. Because the test provides a deterministic `line: (n) => ({ from: (n - 1) * 20 })` and `character: 0`, the anchor is knowable: line=4 (LSP) → codemirror line 5 → `from = 80`, plus character 0 → anchor=80. Replace `expect.any(Number)` with `80` and add a brief comment showing the arithmetic. This turns the test into a true boundary check that would catch off-by-one errors (e.g. `line.number` vs `line.number - 1`).

(S4) In the '.catch() error handler' > 'view.dispatch() throws' test (~lines 436-483), the `withSuppressedRejections` wrapper is misleading because `.catch()` handles the dispatch error synchronously — no unhandled rejection is produced. Either drop the wrapper (call `mousedownHandler(...); await flushMacrotasks();` directly) or add a short comment: `// wrapper is defense-in-depth; .catch() should already handle this`. The same consideration applies to the sibling 'doc.line() throws' test.

Verify with `npm test -- src/__tests__/gotoDefinition.test.ts` in gui/.

Complete in_process_bridge.rs test suite improvements: extract didOpen helper, add didChange/didClose Ok(Value::Null) tests
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1017 review. Two related improvements to `crates/reify-lsp/tests/in_process_bridge.rs`:

**1. Extract didOpen payload helper (reduce duplication)**
The didOpen JSON payload in `did_open_returns_ok_null` (lines 369-398) is an exact duplicate of the payload in `did_open_and_completion_returns_items` (lines 81-91): same URI, same source, same fields. Extract into a test-local helper such as `fn open_bracket_doc_params() -> serde_json::Value` and reuse from both tests so future URI/schema changes live in one place.

**2. Fill Ok(Value::Null) coverage gap for didChange/didClose**
Task 1017 split the old `valid_notification_returns_ok_null` test into `initialized_returns_ok_null` and `did_open_returns_ok_null`, but the original docstring claimed to document the `Ok(Value::Null)` contract for all four notification arms. The refactor leaves `textDocument/didChange` and `textDocument/didClose` with no positive-path return-shape assertion (malformed-params tests exist, but not return-shape). Both arms also return `Ok(Value::Null)` after strict deserialization (see `crates/reify-lsp/src/bridge.rs:129-140`).

Add `did_change_returns_ok_null` and `did_close_returns_ok_null` that perform the minimal handshake + didOpen, then assert each arm returns exactly `Ok(Value::Null)`. Use the extracted didOpen helper from (1).

Source: review suggestions S1 + S2 from reviewer_comprehensive on task 1017 (esc-1017-10).

tasks.title: Reorganize NaN cross-construction assertions between Ord and PartialEq tests
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 911 review triage. Two review suggestions about the NaN assertions added in `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs`:

**S1 (test_organization)** — `value_ord_real_nan_and_neg_zero_still_consistent` (~line 2156) mixes a PartialEq bit-identity assertion (`assert_eq!(nan, nan2, ...)`) into a test whose name/comments focus on Ord/total_cmp. The natural home for a "NaN PartialEq equality" assertion is next to `nan_normalized` (line 1889), which already constructs two independent `f64::NAN` values for a content_hash check.

**S2 (test_coverage)** — After task 911, `value_ord_real_nan_total_order` (~line 2122) still only asserts `nan.cmp(&nan) == Equal` against the *same* binding. There is no assertion that two *independently constructed* NaN values compare Equal under `Ord`. Per the Eq/Ord contract documented at value.rs:983-986 (`a == b` iff `a.cmp(&b) == Ordering::Equal`), this is a coverage gap.

Suggested fix (addresses both):
1. In `value_ord_real_nan_total_order`, add `let nan2 = Value::Real(f64::NAN); assert_eq!(nan.cmp(&nan2), std::cmp::Ordering::Equal);` to lock in the cross-construction Ord invariant.
2. Move the PartialEq assertion out of `value_ord_real_nan_and_neg_zero_still_consistent` into either (a) an extension of `nan_normalized`, or (b) a new sibling test `nan_partialeq_bit_identity` adjacent to `nan_normalized`.

Net effect: Ord checks live with Ord checks, PartialEq checks live with PartialEq checks, and both now cover the cross-construction case.

tasks.title: Sync errorMessage tests between gui/src and gui/sidecar + add no-message-key companion test
tasks.description:
Three related fixes on errorMessage test files, all stemming from review triage of task 1031:

   - Add a new `it('returns "Unknown error" for plain object with undefined .message', ...)` case exercising `errorMessage({ message: undefined })` with a comment explaining that JS `in` returns true for properties set to undefined.
   - The existing `'falls through to String() for plain object without .message'` test at line 145-147 already covers the no-message-key contrast — leave it in place (see item 2 below for the sidecar companion).

2. **Re-add companion no-message-key test in gui/sidecar/src/__tests__/utils.test.ts** — add `it('returns "[object Object]" for plain object with no .message property', () => { expect(errorMessage({})).toBe('[object Object]'); })` adjacent to the new `{ message: undefined }` test (currently lines 58-62). This makes the `'message' in err` vs `err.message === undefined` distinction visible in one place, which is the whole point of the undefined test. The no-key case is currently only covered indirectly by the `{ key: 'val' }` line in the grouped `coerces non-Error, non-string values` test (line 22), which is in a separate describe context.

3. **Remove trailing blank line on gui/sidecar/src/__tests__/utils.test.ts line 67** — stray blank line introduced when the bare-object test was deleted in task 1031. Minor style fix.

tasks.title: Tighten and document meta-test test_self_read_paths_use_manifest_dir (follow-up to 1039)
tasks.description:
Follow-up to task 1039 addressing four reviewer_comprehensive suggestions on `tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs` (in and around `test_self_read_paths_use_manifest_dir`, lines ~1041-1085):

**S1 — stale_comment (line ~1043)**: Update the doc comment that still says "the two source-self-inspection tests" to "each source-self-inspection test" (or "the three") so it stays truthful as the target list grows.

**S2 — hidden_coupling (line ~1045)**: The comment was intentionally single-quoted (`'tests/build_logic_tests.rs'`) so the blanket guard at line ~1080 does not match its own describing comment. This reason is load-bearing but undocumented — a future normalization back to double quotes will make the test fail with a confusing error. Add a brief aside, e.g. `// (single-quoted on purpose so the blanket-guard assertion below does not match this comment)`.

**S3 — brittle_assertion (line ~1068)**: The per-function needle `read_to_string(THIS_FILE)` unnecessarily couples the guard to one read method. A future self-reading test that uses `std::fs::read(THIS_FILE)`, `File::open(THIS_FILE)`, or `fs::read_to_string(Path::new(THIS_FILE))` would fail even though correct. Loosen to `body.contains("(THIS_FILE)")` (still rules out bare-identifier mentions in comments).

**S4 — hardcoded_list (lines ~1059-1063)**: The per-function loop hardcodes three target function signatures. A new self-reading test added later will only be caught by the blanket guard — no per-function safety net. This file already has precedent for dynamic discovery in `find_cfg_unix_test_fns` (~line 785). Consider discovering self-reading tests dynamically: scan for test fns whose body contains `read_to_string(` (or more broadly `(THIS_FILE)` after S3) and assert each uses THIS_FILE. If dynamic discovery is overkill, at minimum add a comment instructing maintainers to append to `target_fns` when adding a self-reading test.

**Scope**: single file, `tree-sitter-reify/tests/build_logic_tests.rs`. Verify with `cargo test -p tree-sitter-reify --test build_logic_tests`.

Source: review triage of task 1039 (esc-1039-15).

tasks.title: Clean up add_line_pair tests and docstrings (follow-up to task 960)
tasks.description:
Follow-up review suggestions from task 960 (add_line_pair doc/test improvements). All changes in crates/reify-constraints/src/solvespace.rs.

1. **Delete add_line_pair_returns_named_line_pair_entities_struct** (S2). It asserts `pair.line_a != pair.line_b` with identical geometry to the pre-existing `add_line_pair_returns_two_line_entities`. The struct's named-field guarantee is enforced at compile time (the test wouldn't compile if the return type became a tuple), so this test adds no runtime coverage. Deleting it also resolves S1 (its stale docstring still references "step 6" and "will FAIL against the current tuple-returning implementation", which is now historical).

2. **Delete add_line_pair_propagates_point_error** (S4). It only exercises `line_a.start`; the new `add_line_pair_propagates_error_from_each_position` subsumes it by testing all four `?` sites.

3. **Add test-fixture helpers near missing_coord_setup** (S3): `fn fixed_point(x: f64, y: f64, z: f64) -> PointRef { PointRef::Fixed { x, y, z } }` and `fn line(start: PointRef, end: PointRef) -> LineRef { LineRef { start, end } }`. Use them in all add_line_pair tests to cut the ~6 repeated 8-field literal blocks.

4. **Rename or weaken add_line_pair_err_leaves_prior_points_registered** (S6). The current test hard-asserts `entities.len() == initial + 1`, encoding "no rollback" as an invariant. If a future refactor adds rollback (which the docstring acknowledges as a defensible improvement), this test would look like a regression rather than an expected inversion. Rename to `add_line_pair_currently_does_not_rollback_on_err` and add a comment that it documents observed behavior, not a requirement; or weaken to `assert!(builder.entities.len() <= initial_entity_count + 1)`.

5. **Tighten add_line_pair docstring dedup paragraph** (S5). Current wording says "if two of the four points have identical coordinates (or the same Auto cell IDs)" — but `PointKey::Auto` hashes the full `(x_id, y_id, z_id)` triple, so two Auto points dedupe only when **all three** cell IDs match. Dedup also applies when more than two points coincide. Rewrite to: "Endpoints with byte-identical Fixed coordinates, or with all three Auto cell IDs equal, are deduplicated through add_point's point_entities cache. The actual point-entity count is therefore between 1 and 4."

After changes: `cargo test -p reify-constraints` and `cargo clippy -p reify-constraints --all-targets -- -D warnings` must be clean.

tasks.title: Enhance assert_orientation_approx! Test Coverage and Reduce Duplication
tasks.description: Improve test coverage and eliminate duplication in assert_orientation_approx! tests in crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs.
tasks.details:
1. **Consolidate Diagnostic Tests:** Replace the duplicated tests `orient_identity_per_component_diagnostic`, `orient_per_component_diagnostic_x`, `_y`, `_z` with a single table-driven test or a helper closure. This should iterate over components `[('w:', factory_w), ('x:', factory_x), ...]` and use `catch_unwind` to handle each case. Aim for a concise implementation of about 20-30 lines.

2. **Explicit-Tolerance Coverage:** Add tests for the explicit-tolerance arm of `assert_orientation_approx!`. Include a positive test case that fails at the default tolerance but passes with a looser tolerance (e.g., `tol = 1e-2`), and a negative test case that panics with a tighter tolerance (e.g., `tol = 1e-6`).

3. **Robustness with Non-Trivial Quaternion:** Introduce a test using a fully-populated quaternion `(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5)` and its negation, ensuring they pass with `sign_insensitive = 1e-10`. This ensures comprehensive coverage of all comparison lines.

tasks.title: lsp_bridge_tests.rs: rename null-literal test, pin handler contract, parameterize malformed-JSON cases
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1001 review triage (esc-1001-81). Three related cleanups in gui/src-tauri/src/tests/lsp_bridge_tests.rs, all in the block lines 192-233:

## (1) Rename `lsp_request_impl_accepts_valid_json_null_literal` (S1)
The current name suggests the call succeeds (Ok), but after Task 1001 the body asserts `result.unwrap_err()` must succeed — i.e. the call must return Err. The invariant actually being checked is narrower: 'null' passes the JSON parse step, even if the downstream LSP handler rejects it.

Rename to `lsp_request_impl_null_literal_passes_json_parse_step` (or similar) and update the body comment accordingly. Source: reviewer_comprehensive S1.

## (2) Pin the initialize-handler contract comment (S2)
`unwrap_err()` at line 214 couples the test to the current behavior of the `initialize` LSP handler (that it rejects null params). If the handler is later changed to accept null params, the test will fail via `unwrap_err` panic even though the JSON-parsing behavior being tested is still correct.

Add an explicit assertion comment pinning "initialize rejects null params" as part of the contract being tested, and reference the handler code location. This documents the coupling rather than hiding it. Source: reviewer_comprehensive S2 option (b).

## (3) Parameterize the malformed-JSON cases (S5)
`lsp_request_impl_rejects_empty_string_params` (lines 221-233) is functionally a subset of `lsp_request_impl_rejects_malformed_json_params` (line 193) — both exercise the `serde_json::from_str(&params).map_err(...)` branch and assert the `invalid JSON params` prefix.

Parameterize the malformed-JSON test with a table like `["not json", "", "{", "\"unterminated"]` to cover multiple edge cases in one place, replacing the two separate tests. Source: reviewer_comprehensive S5.

## Verification
Run `cargo test -p <gui-tauri-crate> lsp_request_impl` to confirm all parameterized cases pass.

Source escalation: esc-1001-81.

tasks.title: type_alias_tests.rs: remove redundant NOTE comment after empty-RHS assertion
tasks.description:
Follow-up from Task 1001 review triage (esc-1001-81), reviewer_comprehensive S3.

In `crates/reify-syntax/tests/type_alias_tests.rs` inside `parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic`, Task 1001 added:

```rust
assert!(
    errors.is_empty(),
    "expected no parse errors for empty-RHS recovery, got: {errors:?}"
);
```

…at lines 326-329. The existing NOTE comment at lines 331-333 says the same thing:
```
// NOTE: Tree-sitter silently recovers `type Foo =` without emitting parse errors.
// The `errors` vector is empty — this is expected Tree-sitter behavior, not a bug.
// The test verifies recovery shape, not error detection.
```

The assertion now encodes the fact; the explanatory block is redundant. Delete the 3-line NOTE block (lines 331-333).

## Explicit non-goal
Do NOT weaken the `errors.is_empty()` assertion. Task 1001 explicitly chose to pin this as a contract so that a grammar change emitting parse errors for `type Foo =` would be caught. The reviewer's alternative (b) — weakening to a comment or tolerant form — is rejected by the original task author's intent.

## Verification
`cargo test -p reify-syntax parse_type_alias_empty_rhs_no_panic` passes.

Source escalation: esc-1001-81.

tasks.title: Harden sync_comments_grep meta-test against drift and false positives
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1025 review (esc-1025-24). Three issues in tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh:

S1 (line 29): Section 1 hardcodes its own PATTERN literal rather than exercising the actual regex in tests/sync_comments_test.sh. If the regex drifts, Section 1 keeps passing while the real test silently breaks — exactly what a meta-test should prevent. Fix: extract the literal regex from sync_comments_test.sh at runtime (e.g. `grep -oE '\\^\\[\\[:space:\\]\\]\\*.*\\[\\[:space:\\(\\]' tests/sync_comments_test.sh | head -1`), substitute `${_expr_ref_fn}` with `sanitize_value`, then run the fixture assertions against the extracted pattern.

S3 (lines 71-77): The consistency assertions `! grep -qF '\\b' "$SYNC_TEST"` and `! grep -qF 'grep -P' "$SYNC_TEST"` are file-wide fixed-string searches. A comment like `# POSIX: do not use \\b here` in sync_comments_test.sh would break CI without a real regression. Fix: scope the search to grep invocation lines only, e.g. `! grep -E "^[^#]*grep[[:space:]]+.*\\\\\\\\b" "$SYNC_TEST"`, or positively assert that all `grep -E` patterns in the file contain `[[:space:](]`.

S5 (lines 30-58): Fixture coverage gaps. Add accept case for tab-indented `\\tfn sanitize_value(`, accept case for multi-space `fn   sanitize_value(` and trailing-space `fn sanitize_value (`, and reject cases for `fnsanitize_value(` (no space) and `my_fn sanitize_value(` — to pin the anchoring behavior.

tasks.title: Widen fn-existence regex in sync_comments_test.sh to cover all Rust fn forms
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1025 review (esc-1025-24, suggestion S2, tests/sync_comments_test.sh:36,48).

The current regex `^[[:space:]]*(pub[[:space:]]+)?(async[[:space:]]+)?fn[[:space:]]+NAME[[:space:](]` silently fails to match several legitimate Rust fn forms:
  - `pub(crate) fn ...`, `pub(super) fn ...`
  - `unsafe fn ...`
  - `const fn ...`
  - generic declarations `fn name<T>(...)` (the `<` is not in `[[:space:](]`)

Currently sanitize_value is plain `fn sanitize_value(`, so this works. But if a future edit adds any of the above forms, the existence assertion will silently pass as a no-op (the name is found by extraction but not by the regex) or fail cryptically. Since this is the exact kind of brittleness task 1025 aims to prevent, widen the pattern now.

Suggested pattern:
`^[[:space:]]*(pub(\\([^)]*\\))?[[:space:]]+)?(unsafe[[:space:]]+)?(const[[:space:]]+)?(async[[:space:]]+)?fn[[:space:]]+NAME[[:space:](<]`

Add corresponding fixture cases in tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh for `pub(crate) fn`, `unsafe fn`, `const fn`, and `fn name<T>(`. Verify with `bash tests/sync_comments_test.sh` and `bash tests/infra/test_sync_comments_grep.sh`.

tasks.title: Remove redundant `gui/node_modules` line from .gitignore
tasks.description: In `.gitignore` line 36, delete the explicit `gui/node_modules` entry. Line 11 already contains `node_modules/`, a recursive pattern that matches any `node_modules` directory at any depth (including `gui/node_modules`), so the explicit entry is dead weight. Pure cleanup — no functional change.

tasks.title: Wire tests/infra/ meta-tests into orchestrator.yaml test_command via run_all.sh
tasks.description:
Follow-up from task 1025 review (esc-1025-24, suggestion S4). Broader project gap.

Problem: tests/infra/ meta-tests (test_sync_comments_grep.sh, test_portable_sha256.sh, test_portable_timeout.sh, etc.) are not wired into orchestrator.yaml's test_command. The test_command runs `tests/sync_comments_test.sh` explicitly but discovers no infra meta-tests, so they only run during authoring sessions and silently rot afterwards.

Options:
  (a) Add each `bash tests/infra/test_*.sh` invocation individually to orchestrator.yaml's test_command next to the existing sync_comments_test.sh line. Simple but doesn't scale.
  (b) Introduce `tests/infra/run_all.sh` — a discovery runner that enumerates `test_*.sh` files in tests/infra/ and runs each with its exit code aggregated. Then replace the explicit sync_comments_test.sh line in orchestrator.yaml with `bash tests/infra/run_all.sh` (or add a new line for it). Document in tests/infra/README.md that all test_*.sh files are auto-discovered.

Prefer option (b). Verify by deliberately breaking a meta-test and confirming the orchestrator verify step catches it.

tasks.title: Harden `gotoDefinition.test.ts` fixtures: delete self-referential tests, convert `BASE_MOUSE_EVENT` to factory, document merge semantics
tasks.description:
In `gui/src/__tests__/gotoDefinition.test.ts`, apply three related fixture cleanups that arose from the Task 1029 review:

(1) Delete the `describe('test fixtures')` block at lines ~43–84. Its sub-tests assert `BASE_MOUSE_EVENT.ctrlKey === true` and `makeMockView().state.doc.lines === 100` — literal echoes of how the fixtures are defined a few lines above. They exercise no production code and add maintenance friction (any intentional default change requires updating both the fixture and its mirror tests). This also removes the misleading `line(0)` call on line 60 (CodeMirror's `doc.line()` is 1-indexed; `line(0)` would throw `RangeError` on a real view — it only passes because the stub ignores its argument), making the S4 concern moot.

(2) Replace the module-level `BASE_MOUSE_EVENT` constant (lines ~34–39) with a `makeMouseEvent(overrides?: Partial<MouseEvent>): MouseEvent` factory that returns a fresh object per call. Rationale: a shared mutable object can leak state across tests if any future test mutates a field (e.g. `mockEvent.ctrlKey = false` to exercise the non-modifier path). This matches the `makeMockView` factory convention, which was deliberately introduced to give each test a fresh `dispatch` spy. Update all ~7 call sites from `BASE_MOUSE_EVENT` to `makeMouseEvent()`.

(3) Add a short JSDoc comment on `makeMockView` documenting the merge semantics, e.g.: "Overrides are merged per-leaf: only the fields you pass are replaced; sibling defaults (e.g. `lineAt` when only `line` is overridden) are preserved." This makes the non-obvious shallow-per-leaf behavior explicit for future callers.

All three changes touch the same file and are logically grouped. No production code is affected; run `npm test -- gotoDefinition` (or the project's equivalent vitest invocation) to verify.

tasks.title: Align concurrent_eval doc comments: add helper reference and update struct doc

2. **Update struct-level doc (lines 51-53):** The `ConcurrentEvalAdapter` struct doc still says "All lock acquisitions recover gracefully from poisoning" — phrased from the implementation-detail angle that the module-level doc just moved away from. tasks.title: Gate debug_assert-dependent test on debug builds
tasks.description:
The test `event_panics_on_non_warn_when_dispatcher_contract_violated` in `crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs` (line ~568) relies on `debug_assert_eq!` to fire a panic, validated via `#[should_panic(expected = "enabled() contract violated")]`. In release builds, `debug_assert_eq!` is compiled out, no panic occurs, and the `#[should_panic]` attribute causes the test to fail.

Fix: Add `#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), ignore)]` (or `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]` above `#[test]`) so the test is skipped in release mode rather than failing. Add a brief inline comment explaining why.

This was identified during review of task 972.

tasks.title: Deduplicate forwarding Subscriber wrappers in tracing_support tests
tasks.description:
The test module in `crates/reify-test-support/src/tracing_support.rs` contains three nearly-identical `tracing::Subscriber` wrapper structs:

1. `EventDispatchCounter<S>` (lines ~230-266) — used by `error_events_rejected_by_enabled_filter`
2. `EventDispatchCounter<S>` (lines ~488-534) — used by `counting_subscriber_enabled_rejects_unregistered_levels`
3. `ForcedEventDispatcher<S>` (lines ~571-606) — used by `event_panics_on_non_warn_when_dispatcher_contract_violated`

Each forwards all trait methods to an inner subscriber and differs only in `enabled()` / `event()` behavior. The boilerplate is substantial and obscures per-test intent.

Extract a single shared `ForwardingSubscriber<S>` at module scope (or a macro) that handles the forwarding boilerplate and allows tests to customize only `enabled()` and `event()` via closures, generics, or trait specialization. This is test-only code, so the goal is purely maintainability.

This was identified during review of task 972.

tasks.title: Fix misleading stdlib-blame diagnostic for cross-module unit collisions
tasks.description:
The duplicate-unit branch in compile_with_prelude uses `original.span == SourceSpan::empty(0)` as a sentinel for 'came from stdlib prelude', but after Task 209 user dependency pub units are also seeded via seed_prelude_unit with SourceSpan::empty(0). This causes collisions with user dep pub units to produce a misleading diagnostic wrongly blaming the stdlib.

Fix: Distinguish stdlib-seeded units from user-import-seeded units by adding provenance (e.g. source_module: Option<String>) to UnitEntry/CompiledUnit. Emit 'already defined in module X' for user imports, keep 'stdlib prelude' only for actual stdlib.

Also tighten test `compile_project_detects_cross_module_unit_collision` to assert: (a) diagnostic mentions the dep module name, (b) does NOT contain "stdlib", (c) error span points at the user's redeclaration.

This localizes the import filter and cloning policy, preventing drift if prelude collection ever needs to handle qualified/glob/aliased imports.

tasks.title: Detect sibling prelude unit collisions and add transitive/diamond test coverage
tasks.description:
Two related issues from Task 209 review:

1. **Silent overwrite (S4):** seed_prelude_unit has last-wins semantics. If entry imports two siblings (A, B) that both declare `pub unit foo` with different definitions, whichever was inserted last wins silently with no diagnostic. Detect this and emit a diagnostic for conflicting units from different prelude modules.

2. **Missing test coverage (S5):** Add tests for:
   - Transitive visibility: entry imports A, A imports B with pub unit → unit should NOT propagate to entry (locks in design choice)
   - Diamond case: A imports B and C, both pub-export same unit → should trigger collision diagnostic (pairs with fix above)
   - Pub unit from dep that shadows a stdlib unit name

tasks.title: Document NaN hash-equality invariant exception with rationale and caller guidance
tasks.description:
In `crates/reify-types/src/value.rs` lines 981-984, the NaN-payload asymmetry paragraph states that NaN-payload differences "affect only PartialEq, not content-addressing" but doesn't acknowledge this is a deliberate violation of the hash-equality invariant (a != b yet content_hash(a) == content_hash(b)).

Add documentation that:
1. Explicitly names this as an exception to the hash-equality invariant enforced elsewhere (see `scalar_neg_zero_hash_differs_from_pos_zero` at ~line 1868, `hash_equality_invariant_real` at ~line 1884)
2. Explains the rationale: NaN payloads are collapsed so semantically-equivalent NaNs from different sources deduplicate in content-addressed storage
3. Provides caller guidance: content-addressed lookups for NaN-bearing values should treat hash-hits as "possibly equal" and re-check PartialEq if exact identity matters
4. Cross-references the existing -0.0/+0.0 invariant tests

Struct-shaped wrappers (Frame, Transform, BoundingBox) have different field structures (origin/basis, rotation/translation) whose delegation paths differ from simple `Vec<Value>` wrappers. tasks.title: Consolidate errorMessage() helper between gui and sidecar copies
tasks.description:
Deferred from task 1048 review suggestion S2 (reviewer_comprehensive).

Two copies of `errorMessage()` are explicitly kept in sync via NOTE comments:
- gui/src/utils/errorClassifier.ts
- gui/sidecar/src/utils.ts

Every hardening change (null/undefined guards, try/catch widening) must be applied identically to both copies. Task 1048 demonstrated the drift risk — its review flagged test-coverage drift between the two suites and the repeated duplication pattern.

**Scope**: Extract `errorMessage()` into a shared module that both the main gui bundle and the sidecar bundle import. The sidecar bundles separately from gui so this needs either (a) a workspace utility package both can depend on, (b) a build-time copy step, or (c) a TypeScript path alias + bundler config. Option (a) is cleanest if the project's npm workspace supports it.

**Out of scope**: Any other duplicated helpers between gui and sidecar — this task targets errorMessage only.
